React Scheduler Component
=========================

![React Scheduler Component (DayPilot)](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/otobaivptjd6jas5cklrghajia/React+Scheduler+Component+%28DayPilot%29.png "React Scheduler Component (DayPilot)")

Open-Source Tutorials
---------------------

*   [React Scheduler with Horizontal Timeline (Open-Source)](https://code.daypilot.org/79813/react-scheduler-with-horizontal-timeline-open-source)
      
    A lightweight, open-source React Scheduler component with a horizontal timeline, drag-and-drop support, customizable appearance, and smooth performance for large data sets.
    
*   [Using React Scheduler with TypeScript](https://code.daypilot.org/34946/react-scheduler-typescript-project)
      
    A sample React project that uses the Scheduler component in TypeScript.
    
*   [React Scheduler: Rendering JSX in Events](https://code.daypilot.org/50584/react-scheduler-rendering-jsx-in-events)
      
    How to render custom JSX in events displayed by the React Scheduler component.
    
*   [React JSX Components in Scheduler Grid Cells](https://code.daypilot.org/43458/react-jsx-components-in-scheduler-grid-cells)
      
    How to render custom React JSX component in the Scheduler grid cells.
    
*   [React Scheduler: Next/Previous Buttons](https://code.daypilot.org/64293/react-scheduler-next-previous-buttons)
      
    Add "next" and "previous" buttons to your React application and let users switch the month displayed by the Scheduler component.
    
*   [React Scheduler: How to Enable Undo/Redo](https://code.daypilot.org/30113/react-scheduler-how-to-enable-undo-redo)
      
    The React Scheduler component can record all drag and drop actions and allow unlimited undo/redo. See how to add undo/redo buttons and display the full history.
    

Tutorials
---------

*   [React Scheduler Tutorial](https://code.daypilot.org/77607/react-scheduler-tutorial)
      
    Introduction to using the React Scheduler component in your application
    
*   [React Shift Scheduling Application (PHP/MySQL Backend)](https://code.daypilot.org/40087/react-shift-scheduling-application-php-mysql-backend)
      
    React application for planning employee shifts at multiple locations.
    
*   [React Activity Planning System (Node, Express, PostgreSQL)](https://code.daypilot.org/95340/react-activity-planning-system-node-express-postgresql)
      
    An application for scheduling activities for different departments. It uses the React Scheduler component to display tasks and milestones.
    
*   [React Work Order Planning System (PHP/MySQL)](https://code.daypilot.org/47819/react-work-order-planning-system-php-mysql)
      
    An application that lets you assign tasks to employees. Includes a React queue component that displays a list of tasks that were not scheduled yet.
    
*   [React Scheduler: Rendering JSX Components in Row Header](https://code.daypilot.org/18688/react-scheduler-rendering-jsx-components-in-row-header)
      
    How to render custom JSX content in Scheduler row headers.
    
*   [React Scheduler: Rendering JSX Components in Callout (Bubble)](https://code.daypilot.org/49524/react-scheduler-rendering-jsx-component-in-callout-bubble)
      
    How to display custom JSX content in hover callouts.
    
*   [React Scheduler: External Drag and Drop](https://code.daypilot.org/85690/react-scheduler-external-drag-and-drop)
      
    How to activate external items so they can be dragged to the Scheduler as events.
    
*   [React Scheduler: Full Screen Layout](https://code.daypilot.org/88196/react-scheduler-full-screen-layout)
      
    Configure the React Scheduler component to fill the available screen space.
    
*   [React Scheduler: How to Display Slot Details using Background Events](https://code.daypilot.org/81940/react-scheduler-how-to-display-slot-details-using-background-events)
      
    Move selected events to the background and use them to display detailed information about a date range.
    
*   [React Scheduler: How to Search Events](https://code.daypilot.org/76269/react-scheduler-how-to-search-events)
      
    How to search React Scheduler events, highlight the search text and jump between results using "Next" and "Previous" buttons.
    
*   [React: Milestone Scheduler](https://code.daypilot.org/31918/react-milestone-scheduler)
      
    The React Scheduler can display milestones in addition to standard events or tasks. You can use custom shapes, icons, colors and connect the milestones using links.
    
*   [Next.js Scheduler Tutorial](https://code.daypilot.org/50496/next-js-scheduler)
      
    How to use the React Scheduler in a Next.js application.
    

What features are available?
----------------------------

To see all the features please see:

*   [Scheduler documentation](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/ "Scheduler Component")
    : The documentation includes detailed description of all the features.
    
*   [Live demos](https://javascript.daypilot.org/demo/scheduler/)
    : The demos are built using the pure JavaScript version of the Scheduler but all the features are also available in the React version.
    

How to install the React Scheduler component?
---------------------------------------------

### DayPilot Lite (Open-Source)

The [open-source](https://javascript.daypilot.org/open-source/)
 version of the React Scheduler component is available in DayPilot Lite for React ([@daypilot/daypilot-lite-react](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@daypilot/daypilot-lite-react)
).

npm install @daypilot/daypilot-lite-react

### DayPilot Pro

You can get the **daypilot-pro-react** module at [npm.daypilot.org](https://npm.daypilot.org/)
:

npm install https://npm.daypilot.org/daypilot-pro-react/trial/2025.3.6519.tar.gz

This package is a part [DayPilot Pro for JavaScript](https://javascript.daypilot.org/)
, a library of advanced scheduling components for web applications.

How to add the React Scheduler component to your application?
-------------------------------------------------------------

You can use the Scheduler component by simply including `<DayPilotScheduler>` tag in your JSX.

### DayPilot Lite (Open-Source)

import React from 'react';
import {DayPilotCalendar} from "@daypilot/daypilot-lite-react";

const Scheduler = () => {
    return (
        <DayPilotScheduler />
    );
}

export default Scheduler;

### DayPilot Pro

import React from 'react';
import {DayPilotScheduler} from "daypilot-pro-react";

const Scheduler = () => {
    return (
        <DayPilotScheduler />
    );
}

export default Scheduler;

How to configure the React Scheduler?
-------------------------------------

![react scheduler component configuration resources](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/neqhctst6bcvtis7en6mk5rc24/react-scheduler-component-configuration-resources.png "react scheduler component configuration resources")

The React Scheduler allows you to customize the appearance and behavior using configuration [properties](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-properties/)
. All properties that are mentioned in the [Scheduler JavaScript API reference](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-class/)
 are available in React as well.

You can specify the properties directly using `<DayPilotScheduler>` attributes:

import React from 'react';
import {DayPilotScheduler} from "daypilot-pro-react";

const Scheduler = () => {
    return (
        <div>
            <DayPilotScheduler
                startDate={"2024-12-01"}
                days={31}
                scale={"Day"}
                timeHeaders={\[{groupBy: "Month"}, {groupBy: "Day", format: "d"}\]}
                resources={\[\
                    {name: "Resource 1", id: "R1"},\
                    {name: "Resource 2", id: "R2"},\
                    {name: "Resource 3", id: "R3"},\
                \]}
            />
        </div>
    );
}

export default Scheduler;

You can also load the properties from the `state` object using [destructuring assignment](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Operators/Destructuring_assignment)
:

import React, { useState } from 'react';
import {DayPilotScheduler} from "daypilot-pro-react";

const Scheduler = () => {
    const \[config, setConfig\] = useState({
        startDate: "2024-12-01",
        days: 31,
        scale: "Day",
        timeHeaders: \[\
            { groupBy: "Month" },\
            { groupBy: "Day", format: "d" }\
        \],
        resources: \[\
            { name: "Resource 1", id: "R1" },\
            { name: "Resource 2", id: "R2" },\
            { name: "Resource 3", id: "R3" },\
        \]
    });

    return (
        <div\>
            <DayPilotScheduler {...config} />
        </div\>
    );
}

export default Scheduler;

How to access the DayPilot.Scheduler API?
-----------------------------------------

![react scheduler component message](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/lwoi4bxcezhspdvmpknw74ojvy/react-scheduler-component-message.png "react scheduler component message")

In addition to automatic change detection available in React you can also use the direct API [methods](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-methods/)
 to control the React Scheduler behavior.

In order to get access to the methods, you need a reference to [DayPilot.Scheduler](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-class/)
 object. The Scheduler object is accessible using `control` property of the `DayPilotScheduler` React component class.

The following example stores the `DayPilot.Scheduler` object as `this.scheduler` property of the Scheduler component:

import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { DayPilotScheduler } from "daypilot-pro-react";

const Scheduler = () => {
    const \[scheduler, setScheduler\] = useState();

    return (
        <div\>
            <DayPilotScheduler
                controlRef\={setScheduler}
            />
        </div\>
    );
}

export default Scheduler;

Now you can invoke the Scheduler methods directly. This example displays a "Welcome!" message using the [message()](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-message/)
 method of the Scheduler when you click the button:

import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { DayPilotScheduler } from "daypilot-pro-react";

const Scheduler = () => {
    const \[scheduler, setScheduler\] = useState();

    return (
        <div\>
            <button onClick\={e => scheduler?.message('Welcome!')}>Welcome</button\>
            <DayPilotScheduler
                controlRef\={setScheduler}
            />
        </div\>
    );
}

export default Scheduler;

Scheduler Component
===================

![html5 javascript scheduler component](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/em3zd7jbwfey3jb4rrjw3yranu/html5-javascript-scheduler-component.png "html5 javascript scheduler component")

The **Scheduler** component displays a timeline for multiple resources.

*   [Appearance](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/appearance/ "Appearance")

     ([Angular](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/angular/ "Angular")

    , [Vue](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/vue-js/ "Vue")
     and others)

    JavaScript, Angular, React and Vue

Width
=====

The `width` property is optional. A `null` value corresponds to the `display:block` style.

You can use any value acceptable for the `width` CSS style.

### JavaScript Example

<div id\="dps"\></div\>
<script type\="text/javascript"\>
  var dps = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dps");
  dps.width = "100%";
  // ...
  dps.init();
</script\>

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

Example:

<DayPilot:DayPilotScheduler 
  runat\="server" 
  id\="DayPilotScheduler1"
  Width\="100%"
  ...
/>

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

Example:

@Html.DayPilotScheduler("dps", new DayPilotSchedulerConfig {
  BackendUrl = ResolveUrl("~/Scheduler/Backend"),
  Width = "100%",
  ...
})

Java
----

Example:

<div id\="dps"\></div\>
<script type\="text/javascript"\>
  var dps = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dps");
  dps.backendUrl = "${pageContext.request.contextPath}/dps";
  dps.width = "100%";
  // ...
  dps.init();
</script\>

# DayPilot Documentation - Scheduling Components for
    JavaScript, Angular, React and Vue

[![DayPilot](https://static.daypilot.org/img/logo/daypilot-logo-40x40.png)DayPilot](https://www.daypilot.org/)

[Products](https://www.daypilot.org/products/)

[Support](https://www.daypilot.org/support/)

  

[DayPilot Documentation](https://doc.daypilot.org/)

[Scheduler Component](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/ "Scheduler Component")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[![html5 javascript scheduler component](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/u7qloavmiveu5mqilka5skmvnq/html5-javascript-scheduler-component.png "html5 javascript scheduler component")](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/ "Scheduler Component")

DayPilot Scheduler component displays a timeline for multiple resources. Supports AJAX and HTML5. Automatic and manual [localization](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/localization/ "Localization")
. Full [CSS](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/css/ "CSS")
 styling support.

Available for [JavaScript](https://javascript.daypilot.org/scheduler/)
, [Angular](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/angular/ "Angular")
, [React](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/react/ "React")
, [Vue.js](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/vue-js/ "Vue")
, [jQuery](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/jquery/ "jQuery")
, [AngularJS 1.x](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/angularjs/ "AngularJS")
, [PHP](https://code.daypilot.org/50604/ajax-scheduler-for-javascript-php)
, [ASP.NET Core](https://code.daypilot.org/99410/asp-net-core-scheduler)
, [Node.js](https://code.daypilot.org/95340/react-activity-planning-system-node-express-postgresql)
, [ASP.NET WebForms](https://aspnet.daypilot.org/scheduler/)
, [ASP.NET MVC](https://mvc.daypilot.org/scheduler/)
, [Java](https://java.daypilot.org/scheduler/)
 (servlets), [Spring](https://code.daypilot.org/70997/using-javascript-html5-scheduler-in-spring-boot-java)
.

[Gantt Chart Component](https://doc.daypilot.org/gantt/ "Gantt Chart Component")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[![html5 javascript gantt chart component](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/4oqhkt72qfggvlmwa35milrzsu/html5-javascript-gantt-chart-component.png "html5 javascript gantt chart component")](https://doc.daypilot.org/gantt/ "Gantt Chart Component")

DayPilot Gantt chart component displays a timeline for a hierarchy of tasks (one task per row).

Available for [JavaScript](https://javascript.daypilot.org/gantt/)
, [Angular](https://doc.daypilot.org/gantt/angular/ "Angular")
, [React](https://doc.daypilot.org/gantt/react/ "React")
, [Vue.js](https://code.daypilot.org/80967/vue-js-gantt-chart-tutorial)
, [jQuery](https://doc.daypilot.org/gantt/jquery/ "jQuery")
, [AngularJS 1.x](https://doc.daypilot.org/gantt/angularjs/ "AngularJS")
, [PHP](https://code.daypilot.org/93862/html5-gantt-chart)
, [ASP.NET WebForms](https://aspnet.daypilot.org/gantt/)
, [ASP.NET MVC](https://mvc.daypilot.org/gantt/)
, [Java](https://java.daypilot.org/gantt/)
 (servlets), [Spring](https://code.daypilot.org/17411/using-javascript-html5-gantt-chart-in-spring-boot-java)
.

[Event Calendar Component](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/ "Event Calendar Component")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[![html5 javascript event calendar component](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/vkacnlej7ndc3hejxunkzpeh4a/html5-javascript-event-calendar-component.png "html5 javascript event calendar component")](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/ "Event Calendar Component")

DayPilot Calendar component displays a [daily](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/day-view/ "Day View")
 and [weekly](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/week-view/ "Week View")
 event calendar. Supports AJAX and HTML5. Automatic and manual [localization](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/localization/ "Localization")
. Full [CSS](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/css/ "CSS")
 styling support.

Available for [JavaScript](http://javascript.daypilot.org/calendar/)
, [Angular](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/angular/ "Angular")
, [React](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/react/ "React")
, [Vue.js](https://code.daypilot.org/10748/vue-js-weekly-calendar-tutorial)
, [jQuery](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/jquery/ "jQuery")
, [AngularJS](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/angularjs/ "AngularJS")
, [PHP](https://code.daypilot.org/47550/event-calendar-widget-for-javascript-php)
, [ASP.NET Core](https://code.daypilot.org/31735/javascript-weekly-calendar-asp-net-core)
, [ASP.NET WebForms](http://www.daypilot.org/calendar/)
, [ASP.NET MVC](http://mvc.daypilot.org/calendar/)
, [Java](http://java.daypilot.org/calendar/)
 (servlets), [Spring](https://code.daypilot.org/41760/using-javascript-html5-event-calendar-in-spring-boot-java)
.

[Monthly Calendar Component](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/ "Monthly Event Calendar Component")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[![html5 javascript monthly calendar component](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/3lumfyfl4jbmhdh6p65tvt3lem/html5-javascript-monthly-calendar-component.png "html5 javascript monthly calendar component")](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/ "Monthly Event Calendar Component")

DayPilot Month component displays a monthly event calendar. Supports AJAX and HTML5. Automatic and manual [localization](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/localization/ "Localization")
. Full [CSS](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/css/ "CSS")
 styling support.

Available for [JavaScript](http://javascript.daypilot.org/month/)
, [Angular](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/angular/)
, [React](https://code.daypilot.org/26289/react-monthly-calendar-tutorial)
, Vue.js, [jQuery](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/jquery/ "jQuery")
, [AngularJS](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/angularjs/ "AngularJS")
, [PHP](https://code.daypilot.org/57519/monthly-event-calendar-for-javascript-html5-php)
, [ASP.NET Core](https://code.daypilot.org/95109/html5-monthly-calendar-and-asp-net-core)
, [ASP.NET WebForms](http://www.daypilot.org/month/)
, [ASP.NET MVC](http://mvc.daypilot.org/month/)
, [Java](http://java.daypilot.org/month/)
 (servlets), [Spring](https://code.daypilot.org/58614/using-javascript-html5-monthly-calendar-in-spring-boot-java)
.

[Kanban Component](https://doc.daypilot.org/kanban/ "Kanban Component")

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[![html5 javascript kanban board component](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/ffhsd6v7bbg5hbbg7cxuhnjoiy/html5-javascript-kanban-board-component.png "html5 javascript kanban board component")](https://doc.daypilot.org/kanban/ "Kanban Component")

DayPilot Kanban component displays tasks arranged by state in columns.

# Height - Scheduler | DayPilot Documentation - Scheduling Components for
    JavaScript, Angular, React and Vue

    *   [Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/ "Scheduler Component")
        *   [Appearance](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/appearance/ "Appearance")

Height
======

The [Scheduler component](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/ "Scheduler Component")
 supports several modes for specifying the height (`heightSpec` property):

*   **Max** (default; the height will grow as in Auto mode up to the value specified in `Height` property; than the height will be fixed and scrollbar added)
    
*   **Auto** (the height will be determined by row heights, no scrollbar is added)
    
*   **Fixed** (the `Height` property value will be used, scrollbar is added when necessary)
    
*   **Parent100Pct** (the height will be set to 100% of the parent element)
    
*   **Max100Pct** (the height will grow until 100% of the parent element is reached)
    

The [Lite edition](https://javascript.daypilot.org/open-source/)
 supports `"Auto"`, `"Max"`, and `"Fixed"` modes.

### JavaScript Scheduler

The [JavaScript Scheduler](https://javascript.daypilot.org/scheduler/)
 lets you configure the height using `heightSpec` and `height` properties:

<div id\="scheduler"\></div\>
<script\>
  const scheduler = new DayPilot.Scheduler("scheduler", {
    heightSpec: "Max",
    height: 400,
    // ...
  });
  scheduler.init();
</script\>

### React Scheduler

The height of the [React Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/react/ "React")
 component can be specified using `heightSpec` and `height` attributes of the `<DayPilotScheduler>` tag:

<DayPilotScheduler
  heightSpec\={"Max"}
  height\={250}
/>

The [React Scheduler: Full Screen Layout](https://code.daypilot.org/88196/react-scheduler-full-screen-layout)
 tutorial displays a React Scheduler set to fill the available vertical space using `heightSpec={"Parent100Pct"}`. You can use this setting to integrate the Scheduler into applications that use a single screen UI without a vertical scrollbar.

### Angular Scheduler

The [Angular Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/angular/ "Angular")
 component height can be configured using `heightSpec` and `height` properties of the config object.

import {Component, ViewChild, AfterViewInit} from '@angular/core';
import {DayPilot, DayPilotSchedulerComponent} from 'daypilot-pro-angular';

@Component({
  selector: 'scheduler-component',
  template: \`<daypilot-scheduler \[config\]="config"></daypilot-scheduler>\`,
  styles: \[\`\`\]
})
export class SchedulerComponent implements AfterViewInit {

  config: DayPilot.SchedulerConfig = {
    heightSpec: "Max"
    height: 500,
    // ...
  };

}

See the [Angular Scheduler: Full Screen Layout](https://code.daypilot.org/75104/angular-scheduler-full-screen-layout)
 tutorial and download an Angular project that shows how to set the Angular Scheduler height to 100% of the parent element.

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

Example:

<DayPilot:DayPilotScheduler 
  runat\="server" 
  id\="DayPilotScheduler1"
  HeightSpec\="Max"
  Height\="250"
  ...
/>

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

Example:

@Html.DayPilotScheduler("dps", new DayPilotSchedulerConfig {
  BackendUrl = ResolveUrl("~/Scheduler/Backend"),
  Height = 300,
  HeightSpec = HeightSpec.Max,
  ...
})

Java
----

Example:

<div id\="dps"\></div\>
<script type\="text/javascript"\>
  var dps = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dps");
  dps.backendUrl = "${pageContext.request.contextPath}/dps";
  dps.heightSpec = "Max";
  dps.height = 250;
  // ...
  dps.init();
</script\>

Scrollbar Position
==================

JavaScript
----------

The following methods are available:

*   [getViewPort](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-getviewport/ "DayPilot.Scheduler.getViewPort")
    ()
    
*   [getScrollX](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-getscrollx/ "DayPilot.Scheduler.getScrollX")
    ()
    
*   [getScrollY](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-getscrolly/ "DayPilot.Scheduler.getScrollY")
    ()
    
*   [scrollTo](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-scrollto/)
    (date)
    
*   [scrollToResource](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-scrolltoresource/)
    (id)
    
*   [setScroll](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-setscroll/ "DayPilot.Scheduler.setScroll")
    (scrollX, scrollY)
    
*   [setScrollX](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-setscrollx/)
    (scrollX)
    
*   [setScrollY](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-setscrolly/)
    (scrollY)
    

Examples
--------

Scroll one month forward.

const start = dp.getViewPort().start;
dps.scrollTo(start.addMonths(1));

Scroll one month backward.

const start = dps.getViewPort().start;
dp.scrollTo(start.addMonths(-1));

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

The Scheduler scrollbar position can be read and changed using the following properties:

*   ScrollX
    
*   ScrollY
    

Both properties work with the scrollbar position **in pixels**. The scrollbar position can be set during initial page load, during PostBacks and CallBacks. Changing the scrollbar position during CallBack causes a [full update](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/callback-update/ "CallBack Update")
 (Update() call is required).

In addition, it's possible to set the horizontal (X) scrollbar position using the following method:

*   SetScrollX(DateTime)
    

SetScrollX calculates the scrollbar position from a given **time point** (position parameter). It works only during the initial page load and during PostBacks.

protected void Page\_Load(object sender, EventArgs e)
{
  if (!IsPostBack)
  {
      DayPilotScheduler1.SetScrollX(DateTime.Today.AddDays(\-1));
      setDataSourceAndBind();
      DayPilotScheduler1.UpdateWithMessage("Welcome!");
  }
}

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

Set/get properties (pixels):

*   ScrollX
    
*   ScrollY
    

Set method (DateTime):

*   ScrollTo(DateTime)
    

Example

protected override void OnInit(InitArgs ea)
{
  ScrollTo(DateTime.Today);
  UpdateWithMessage("Welcome!", CallBackUpdateType.Full);
}

Layout
======

### Table-based layout

The layout of the previous DayPilotScheduler versions (up to 4.9) was based on a <table> - the main table had four cells (two columns, two rows), each containing one element (the upper-left corner, time header, resources header, and the main area). The width of the right column was not specified (so it filled the necessary space):

![table based](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/sjgl5m7w2jg5jknew5derw7emi/table-based.png "table based")

This didn't work for DayPilotScheduler with width specified in percent (rather than pixels) in Safari 3, Google Chrome, and Firefox 3.

### Div-based layout

DayPilotScheduler 5.0 introduced a different layout, based on <div> elements (with float:left specified for the left column):

![div based](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/v5i44xt5lndilgouwchx3bkt2m/div-based.png "div based")

The drawback is that the div-based layout **doesn't work well** (especially with DayPilotScheduler.Width specified in percent rather than pixels) if you place the control inside a table with a column that has no width specified.

![table based master](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/hhbboxnuuna6nfwqbniisjom7m/table-based-master.png "table based master")

That's why the new master page layout of the demo in DayPilot Pro 5.0 is switched to div-based as well.

**Demo.master:**

<div id\="container" style\="width:100%"\>
    <div id\="left" style\="float:left; width: 120px; "\>
        Menu goes here...
    </div\>
    <div id\="main" style\="margin-left: 120px"\>
        <div style\="width:99%"\> <!-- This div fixes IE6 problems -->
            DayPilotScheduler goes here...
        </div\>
    </div\>
</div\>

### "Layout" property

The "Layout" property has three possible values:

**1\. LayoutEnum.Auto (default)**

Corresponds to the behavior introduced in 5.0

*   for IE6 it renders table-based layout
*   for all other browsers it renders div-based layout

**2\. LayoutEnum.DivBased**

Always renders the div-based layout.

**3\. LayoutEnum.TableBased**

Always renders the table-based layout (layout used in 4.9 and previous versions).

Now you can apply your own rules for choosing the layout. Example:

protected void Page\_Load(object sender, EventArgs e)
{
  if (Request.UserAgent.IndexOf("MSIE 7") != \-1 || 
      Request.UserAgent.IndexOf("MSIE 6") != \-1)   {
    DayPilotScheduler1.Layout = LayoutEnum.TableBased;
  }
  else   {
    DayPilotScheduler1.Layout = LayoutEnum.DivBased;
  }
}

Compatibility overview
----------------------

### DayPilotScheduler.Width in percent

|     | Explorer 6 | Explorer 7 | Explorer 8 | Firefox 2 | Firefox 3 | Safari 3 | Google Chrome | Visual Studio 2005 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table-based layout | OK  | OK  | X   | OK  | X   | X   | X   | OK  |
| Div-based layout (inside div-based master page) | X   | OK  | OK  | OK  | OK  | OK  | OK  | X   |
| Div-base layout (inside table-based master page) | X   | X   | OK  | OK  | X   | X   | X   | X   |

### DayPilotScheduler.Width in pixels

|     | Explorer 6 | Explorer 7 | Explorer 8 | Firefox 2 | Firefox 3 | Safari 3 | Google Chrome | Visual Studio 2005 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table-based layout | OK  | OK  | OK  | OK  | OK  | OK  | OK  | OK  |
| Div-based layout (inside div-based master page) | X   | OK  | OK  | OK  | OK  | OK  | OK  | X   |
| Div-base layout (inside table-based master page) | X   | OK  | OK  | OK  | OK  | OK  | OK  | X   |

Examples
--------

### JavaScript Example

<div id\="dps"\></div\>
<script type\="text/javascript"\>
  var dps = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dps");
  dps.layout = "DivBased";
  // ...
  dps.init();
</script\>

### ASP.NET WebForms Example

<DayPilot:DayPilotScheduler 
  runat\="server" 
  id\="DayPilotScheduler1"  Layout\="DivBased"  .../>

### ASP.NET MVC Example

@Html.DayPilotScheduler("dps", new DayPilotSchedulerConfig {
  BackendUrl = ResolveUrl("~/Scheduler/Backend"),
  Layout = LayoutType.DivBased,
  ...
})

### Java Example (JSP)

<div id\="dps"\></div\>
<script type\="text/javascript"\>
  var dps = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dps");
  dps.backendUrl = "${pageContext.request.contextPath}/dps";
  dps.layout = "DivBased";
  // ...
  dps.init();
</script\>

CssOnly Mode
============

Behavior
--------

*   **DayPilot Pro 8.0 and later versions only support CssOnly=true mode.**
*   In the CssOnly mode, it is possible to style all scheduler elements. The legacy mode (CssOnly=false) only supports styling of selected elements. The internal DOM structure is different (especially the event box).
*   CssOnly mode available in [DayPilot 7.0](http://www.daypilot.org/daypilot-pro-for-asp-net-webforms-7-0.html "DayPilot Pro for ASP.NET WebForms 7.0")
     and later versions.
*   In the CssOnly mode, all the class names include a leading underscore (unlike the old mode when CssOnly="false"). Using CssClassPrefix="scheduler\_white" will produce classes like "scheduler\_white\_message". In the CssOnly="false" mode the underscore was part of the custom prefix (CssClassPrefix="scheduler\_silver\_"). This means the old themes are not compatible with CssOnly mode.

Theme Designer
--------------

*   You can create a theme using the online **DayPilot Theme Designer**: [http://themes.daypilot.org/](http://themes.daypilot.org/)

Row Height
==========

By default, the height of Scheduler rows is determined by the `eventHeight` property and the maximum number of overlapping events in the row. That means that when you add new concurrent events the height will increase automatically.

Single Event

![javascript scheduler row height single event](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/gsssuhybwfbqvivvbbdbuacq6y/javascript-scheduler-row-height-single-event.png "javascript scheduler row height single event")

Two Overlapping Events

![javascript scheduler row height overlapping events](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/jjmbmji2urfulnoaqvmfsee2fa/javascript-scheduler-row-height-overlapping-events.png "javascript scheduler row height overlapping events")

### Row Margin

You can set custom top and/or bottom row margin (in pixels):

1\. Global settings: `rowMarginBottom`, `rowMarginTop` properties of the Scheduler component

2\. Per row: `marginBottom`, `marginTop` properties of the resource object.

![javascript scheduler row margin bottom](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/xanuzv45efhy5psn3tobk73y3y/javascript-scheduler-row-margin-bottom.png "javascript scheduler row margin bottom")

### Minimum Row Height

You can specify a minimum row height globally using `rowMinHeight` property (in pixels) or per row using `minHeight` property of the Scheduler resource object.

![javascript scheduler row min height](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/xbvdhlm7drhjnjooulrpbzluve/javascript-scheduler-row-min-height.png "javascript scheduler row min height")

JavaScript
----------

In the [JavaScript Scheduler](https://javascript.daypilot.org/scheduler/)
, use [rowMinHeight](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-rowminheight/)
, [rowMarginTop](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-rowmargintop/)
, and [rowMarginBottom](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-rowmarginbottom/)
 properties. You can also override these global values for individual rows using [resources](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-resources/)
 array items.

<div id\="dp"\></div\>
<script\>
  const dp = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dp", {
    // ...
    rowMinHeight: 60,
  });
  dp.init();
</script\>

You can specify custom `eventHeight` value for rows with no events. This will set the row height accordingly.

dp.resources = \[\
  { name: "Group1", id:"G1", eventHeight: 20}\
\];

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

<DayPilot:DayPilotScheduler runat\="server" id\="DayPilotScheduler1"
  ...
  RowMinHeight = "60"
/>

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

You can adjust the row height manually using Resource properties.

@Html.DayPilotScheduler("dps\_rowheadercolumns", new DayPilotSchedulerConfig {
  BackendUrl = ResolveUrl("~/Scheduler/Backend"),
  Resources = new ResourceCollection {
    new Resource("Room B", "B"),
    new Resource{Name = "Room C", Id = "C", MinHeight = 60 },
    new Resource{Name = "Room D", Id = "D", MarginBottom = 5 },
  }
})

### Minimum Row Height 

![scheduler row minheight](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/pq4tm7c6rrdstduf23p3i4ud5u/scheduler-row-minheight.png "scheduler row minheight")

MinHeight = 60

### Bottom Margin

![scheduler row marginbottom](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/3j6jz26bu5an5kdlplicny4fsu/scheduler-row-marginbottom.png "scheduler row marginbottom")

MarginBottom = 5

Cell Customization
==================

![html5 scheduler cell customization](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/kgubwuwvhfhknavzr5lk5bkbaa/html5-scheduler-cell-customization.png "html5 scheduler cell customization")

The appearance of the [JavaScript Scheduler](https://javascript.daypilot.org/scheduler/)
 grid cells can be customized using **onBeforeCellRender** event handler. 

JavaScript Scheduler
--------------------

The [onBeforeCellRender](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-onbeforecellrender/)
 event is called before a cell is rendered for the first time. You can use the event handler to customize the cell **appearance** (background color, HTML, ...) and **behavior** (add active areas, set disabled status, ...).

### Cell Rendering

Cells are only rendered for the current viewport. Additional cells will be rendered as the user scrolls to new areas.

Rendered cells remain in the DOM, until the DOM cache is full. The DOM cache is enabled by default ([cellSweeping](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-cellsweeping/)
 property) and the cache size is set to 1000 cells ([cellSweepingCacheSize](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-cellsweeping/)
).

All cells in a row are re-rendered when any event in that row gets changed (added, removed, updated).

### Scrolling Performance

The `onBeforeCellRender` event is called many times during scrolling and it is very sensitive to ineffective code. See more in [Scrolling Performance](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/scrolling-performance/ "Scrolling Performance")
.

There is a configurable delay before the cells are rendered during scrolling ([scrollDelayCells](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-scrolldelaycells/)
). By default, the cells are rendered synchronously (the default value is 0). This provides great real-time feedback during scrolling but it may slow down the scrolling if the `onBeforeCellRender` code is heavy.

### Properties Caching

The cell properties you set using `onBeforeCellRender` are cached until the next [update()](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-update/)
 call. Please note that this cache holds the `onBeforeCellRender` results (it means `onBeforeCellRender` will not be called again for this cell) and it is different from the DOM cache mentioned above in the "Cell Rendering" section.

If the cell status depends on the events (e.g. when displaying [resource utilization](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/resource-utilization/ "Resource Utilization")
) you will need to turn the caching off using [beforeCellRenderCaching](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-beforecellrendercaching/)
. This will make sure that `onBeforeCellRender` will be called every time the cell is rendered.

See also the [JavaScript Scheduler: Displaying Group Availability](https://code.daypilot.org/97538/javascript-scheduler-displaying-group-availability)
 tutorial for an example.

### Demo

*   [JavaScript Scheduler Cell Customization Demo](https://javascript.daypilot.org/demo/scheduler/cellcustomization.html)
    

### See Also

*   [DayPilot.Scheduler.onBeforeCellRender](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-onbeforecellrender/)
    
*   [DayPilot.Scheduler.beforeCellRenderCaching](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-beforecellrendercaching/)
    

### Examples

Adding a custom CSS class to Monday cells:

![html5 scheduler grid cell customization](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/ufvyi7tbfnhh3i4i76y7rtdjay/html5-scheduler-grid-cell-customization.png "html5 scheduler grid cell customization")

dp.onBeforeCellRender = function(args) {
  if (args.cell.start.getDayOfWeek() === 1) {
    args.cell.cssClass = "monday";
  }
};

Highlighting Saturday:

![html5 scheduler grid cell highlighting saturday](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/euhcnpxrkzdmfae7gyida2jwue/html5-scheduler-grid-cell-highlighting-saturday.png "html5 scheduler grid cell highlighting saturday")

dp.onBeforeCellRender = function(args) {
  if (args.cell.start.getDayOfWeek() === 6) {
    args.cell.backColor = "#dddddd";
  }
};

[Highlighting today](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/highlighting-today/ "Highlighting Today")
:

![html5 scheduler highlighting today](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/6iodaa62pjbpvcgs5iu2z35hqy/html5-scheduler-highlighting-today.png "html5 scheduler highlighting today")

dp.onBeforeCellRender = function(args) {
  if (args.cell.start <= DayPilot.Date.today() && DayPilot.Date.today() < args.cell.end) {
      args.cell.backColor = "#ffcccc";
  }
};

Custom business hours for a resource (resource "C", from 10 am to 3 pm):

![html5 scheduler custom business hours](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/hlr2yd6zkbbc5gktowy2q5wqnu/html5-scheduler-custom-business-hours.png "html5 scheduler custom business hours")

dp.onBeforeCellRender = function(args) {
  if (args.cell.resource === "C") {
    var hour = args.cell.start.getHours();
    if (10 <= hour && hour < 15) {
      args.cell.business = true;
    }
    else {
      args.cell.business = false;
    }
  }
};

*   [JavaScript Scheduler: Displaying Group Availability](https://code.daypilot.org/97538/javascript-scheduler-displaying-group-availability)

*   [Tutorial: ASP.NET Core Hotel Room Booking App](https://code.daypilot.org/96705/asp-net-core-hotel-room-booking-app)

Angular Scheduler
-----------------

In the [Angular Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/angular/ "Angular")
 component, you can define your own `onBeforeCellRender` event handler using the `config` object:

import {Component, ViewChild} from '@angular/core';
import {DayPilot, DayPilotSchedulerComponent} from 'daypilot-pro-angular';

@Component({
  selector: 'scheduler-component',
  template: \`<daypilot-scheduler \[config\]="config" #scheduler></daypilot-scheduler>\`,
  styles: \[\`\`\]
})
export class SchedulerComponent {

  @ViewChild('scheduler')
  scheduler!: DayPilotSchedulerComponent;

  config: DayPilot.SchedulerConfig = {
    onBeforeCellRender: args => {
      if (args.cell.start <= DayPilot.Date.today()) {
        args.cell.backColor = "#f0f0f0";
      }
    },

    // ...

  };

  // ...

}

### Angular Tutorials

*   [Angular Scheduler: Highlighting Holidays](https://code.daypilot.org/38541/angular-scheduler-highlighting-holidays)
    
*   [Angular Scheduler: Resource Utilization Chart](https://code.daypilot.org/31486/angular-scheduler-resource-utilization-chart)
    

React Scheduler
---------------

Use the `onBeforeCellRender` attribute of the `<DayPilotScheduler>` [React Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/react/ "React")
 component to customize the grid cells:

import React, {Component} from 'react';
import {DayPilot, DayPilotScheduler} from "daypilot-pro-react";

class Scheduler extends Component {

  constructor(props) {
    super(props);
  }

  // ...
  
  onBeforeCellRender(args) {
    if (args.cell.start < DayPilot.Date.today()) {
      args.cell.backColor = "#dd7e6b";
    }  
  }

  render() {
    var {...config} = this.state;
    return (
        <DayPilotScheduler
          onBeforeCellRender={args => this.onBeforeCellRender(args)}
        />
    );
  }
}

export default Scheduler;

### React Tutorials

*   [React JSX Components in Scheduler Grid Cells](https://code.daypilot.org/43458/react-jsx-components-in-scheduler-grid-cells)
    

Vue Scheduler
-------------

In the [Vue Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/vue-js/ "Vue")
 component, use the `onBeforeEventRender` property of the `config` object to specify the event handler:

<template\>
  <DayPilotScheduler id\="dp" :config\="config" ref\="scheduler" />
</template\>

<script\>
import {DayPilot, DayPilotScheduler} from 'daypilot-pro-vue'

export default {
  name: 'Scheduler',
  data: function() {
    return {
      config: {
        onBeforeCellRender: args => {
          if (args.cell.start === DayPilot.Date.today()) {
            args.cell.backColor = "#b6d7a8";
          }
        }
      },
    }
  },
  components: {
    DayPilotScheduler
  },
  
  // ...
}
</script\>

### Vue Scheduler Tutorials

*   [Vue Scheduler: How to Display Holidays](https://code.daypilot.org/40697/vue-scheduler-how-to-display-holidays)
    
*   [Vue Scheduler: Availability/Utilization Histogram](https://code.daypilot.org/18387/vue-scheduler-availability-utilization-histogram)
    

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

Properties supported in `BeforeCellRender`:

*   e.BackgroundImage
    
*   e.BackgroundRepeat
    
*   e.BackgroundColor
    
*   e.IsBusiness
    
*   e.CssClass
    
*   e.Disabled
    
*   e.Html
    

### 1\. Changing IsBusiness Status

You can change the IsBusiness status using **e.IsBusiness** property. The cells will automatically use the color specified for business cell (BackColor) or non-business cells (NonBusinessBackColor).

Example

protected void DayPilotScheduler1\_BeforeCellRender(object sender, DayPilot.Web.Ui.Events.BeforeCellRenderEventArgs e)
{
  if (e.Start.Hour == 13)
  {
    e.IsBusiness = false;
  }
}

### 2\. Changing the Color

You can also change the cell color directly using e.BackgroundColor.

Example

protected void DayPilotScheduler1\_BeforeCellRender(object sender, DayPilot.Web.Ui.Events.BeforeCellRenderEventArgs e)
{
  if (e.ResourceId == "A")
  {
    e.BackgroundColor = "red";
  }
}

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

protected override void  OnBeforeCellRender(BeforeCellRenderArgs e)
{

  // highlight parent resource cells
  if (Resources.FindById(e.ResourceId).Children.Count > 0)
  {
    e.BackgroundColor = "white";
  }

  // highlight first day of month
  if (e.Start.Day == 1)
  {
      e.CssClass = "red";
  }
}

Row Header Customization
========================

The resource (row) headers of the Scheduler component can be customized using the `onBeforeRowHeaderRender` event handler. This event is called once for every row (even when the rows are generated in [Gantt](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/gantt-chart/ "Gantt Chart")
 or [Timesheet](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/timesheet/ "Timesheet")
 mode).

It is possible to change the HTML, configure active areas, and set custom properties such as CSS class, background color, etc.

JavaScript Scheduler
--------------------

The [JavaScript Scheduler](https://javascript.daypilot.org/scheduler/)
 lets you customize the row headers using the [onBeforeRowHeaderRender](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-onbeforerowheaderrender/)
 event:

*   It is fired in real time whenever the row header is actually rendered.
    
*   It can be fired repeatedly as the [row headers are rendered progressively](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/progressive-row-rendering/ "Progressive Row Rendering")
     (and removed if you scroll them out of the viewport).
    
*   You can access the row object here using `args.row` ([DayPilot.Row](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-row-class/)
    ).
    
*   You can also access the related events using [args.row.events.all()](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-row-events-all/)
     or [args.row.events.forRange()](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-row-events-forrange/)
     methods and use this information to show dynamic data (e.g. [availability](https://code.daypilot.org/tutorials/availability)
    ).
    

Example Scheduler config:

{
  onBeforeRowHeaderRender: args => {
    args.row.backColor = "#e0e0e0";
    args.row.columns\[0\].text = args.row.data.description;
  }
}

### Styling Row Headers using Custom CSS

![scheduler row header custom css class](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/kvfvdxyswjbhhmpaaqfetzyium/scheduler-row-header-custom-css-class.png "scheduler row header custom css class")

You can apply custom CSS class to selected rows using onBeforeRowHeaderRender event handler.

CSS

<style\>
  .row1 {
      color: white;
      background: red;
  }
</style\>

JavaScript

dp.onBeforeRowHeaderRender = function(args) {
  args.row.cssClass = "row1";
};

### Adding a Color Bar using Active Areas

![scheduler row header active area bar](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/ppkbsajnvjcmnjkwtox2btc6n4/scheduler-row-header-active-area-bar.png "scheduler row header active area bar")

Global CSS to make space for the bar (when [row header width auto-fit](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/row-header-width-auto-fit/ "Row Header Width Auto-Fit")
 is enabled):

<style\>
    .scheduler\_default\_rowheader\_inner
    {
        padding-right: 10px;
    }
</style\>

Adding a bar using [row header active areas](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/row-header-active-areas/ "Row Header Active Areas")
:

dp.onBeforeRowHeaderRender = function(args) {
  var color = (args.row.index % 2) ? "#cc4125" : "#e69138";   // alternating colors
  args.row.areas = \[\
      { right: 2, top: 2, bottom: 2, width: 6, backColor: color }\
  \];
};

When using [row header columns](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/row-header-columns/ "Row Header Columns")
, it's necessary to add the area to the column object:

dp.rowHeaderColumns = \[\
  { text: "Name", display: "name" },\
  { text: "Capacity", display: "capacity" }\
\];
dp.onBeforeRowHeaderRender = function(args) {
  var color = (args.row.index % 2) ? "#cc4125" : "#e69138"; // alternating colors
  var last = args.row.columns.length - 1;
  args.row.columns\[last\].areas = \[\
    { right: 2, top: 2, bottom: 2, width: 6, backColor: color }\
  \];
};

### JavaScript Scheduler Tutorials

*   [JavaScript Scheduler: Using Checkboxes to Select Rows](https://code.daypilot.org/63820/javascript-scheduler-using-checkboxes-to-select-rows)
    

Angular Scheduler
-----------------

In the [Angular Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/angular/ "Angular")
 component, add a new `onBeforeRowHeaderRender` event handler to the `config` object:

@Component({
  selector: 'scheduler-component',
  template: \`<daypilot-scheduler \[config\]="config"></daypilot-scheduler>\`,
  styles: \[\`\`\]
})
export class TimesheetComponent implements AfterViewInit {

  config: DayPilot.SchedulerConfig = {
    rowHeaderColumns: \[\
      {title: "Name"},\
      {title: "Details"}\
    \],
    onBeforeRowHeaderRender: args => {
      args.row.columns\[1\].text = "Row details";
    },
  };

  // ...

}

### Angular Scheduler Tutorial

*   [Angular Scheduler: Row Header Actions](https://code.daypilot.org/79997/angular-scheduler-row-header-actions)
    
*   [Angular Scheduler: Resource Management](https://code.daypilot.org/64657/angular-scheduler-resource-management)
    
*   [Angular Scheduler: Add Angular Components to Row Headers](https://code.daypilot.org/53927/angular-scheduler-add-angular-components-to-row-headers)
    

Vue Scheduler
-------------

In the [Vue Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/vue-js/ "Vue")
, there are two methods for customizing row headers:

1.  Using the `onBeforeRowHeaderRender` Event Handler
    
2.  Using Vue Templates
    

#### 1\. Using the `onBeforeRowHeaderRender` Event Handler

The `onBeforeRowHeaderRender` event is triggered before each row header is rendered. This event is called once per row, allowing you to modify the properties of the entire row (`args.row`) or individual cells if row header columns are enabled (`args.row.columns`).

Use Cases:

*   Setting the background color (`args.row.bacColor`)
    
*   Applying a CSS class (`args.row.cssClass`)
    
*   Adjusting content alignment (`args.row.verticalAlignment`, `args.row.horizontalAlignment`)
    
*   Defining [active areas](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/row-header-active-areas/ "Row Header Active Areas")
    
*   Modifying behavior (e.g., preventing row movement for specific rows)
    

<template\>
  <DayPilotScheduler
    ...
    :events\="events"
    :resources\="resources"
    :rowHeaderColumns\="\[{ title: 'Name' }, { title: 'Details' }\]"
    @beforeRowHeaderRender\="onBeforeRowHeaderRender"
  />
</template\>

<script setup\>
import { DayPilotScheduler } from 'daypilot-pro-vue';
import { ref } from 'vue';

const events = ref(\[\]);
const resources = ref(\[\
  { id: 1, name: 'Resource 1', details: 'Detail A' },\
  { id: 2, name: 'Resource 2', details: 'Detail B' },\
\]);

const onBeforeRowHeaderRender = (args) => {
  // Set the background color of the entire row
  args.row.backColor = "#e1ac63";

  // Modify the text of the second column, if it exists
  if (args.row.columns.length > 1) {
    args.row.columns\[1\].text = args.row.data.details || "Details...";
  }

  // Example: Prevent moving for a specific row
  if (args.row.data.id === 2) {
    args.row.moveDisabled = true;
  }
};
</script\>

**2\. Using Vue Templates**

To customize the row headers in the Vue Scheduler, you can define a Vue template and assign it to the `#rowHeader` slot. This template is applied to every row header cell.

The `#rowHeader` template receives an `args` object, which provides access to the row and column data:

*   **`row`**: Represents the current row as a `DayPilot.Row` object. This object contains all the properties and data associated with the row.
    
*   **`x`**: Indicates the column index, specifying which row header column is being rendered. This is useful when you have multiple [row header columns](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/row-header-columns/ "Row Header Columns")
     and need to define content for the additional columns.
    

In the template, you can access the original row data (from `resources`) using `args.row.data`.

<template\>
  <DayPilotScheduler
    ...
    :events\="events"
    :resources\="resources"
    :rowHeaderColumns\="rowHeaderColumns"
    @timeRangeSelected\="onTimeRangeSelected"
    @beforeEventRender\="onBeforeEventRender"
  >
    <template #rowHeader\="{ row, x }"\>
      <div class\="custom-row-header"\>
        <!-- First Column: Resource Name -->
        <template v-if\="x === 0"\>
          <strong\>{{ row.name }}</strong\>
        </template\>

        <!-- Second Column: Custom Description -->
        <template v-else-if\="x === 1"\>
          <em\>{{ row.data.description }}</em\>
        </template\>
      </div\>
    </template\>

  </DayPilotScheduler\>
</template\>

<script setup\>
import { ref } from 'vue';
import { DayPilotScheduler, DayPilot } from 'daypilot-pro-vue';

// Sample event data
const events = ref(\[\]);

// Sample resource data with a custom description field
const resources = ref(\[\
  { id: 1, name: 'Resource 1', description: 'Description for Resource 1' },\
  { id: 2, name: 'Resource 2', description: 'Description for Resource 2' },\
\]);

const rowHeaderColumns = ref(\[\
  { title: 'Name' },\
  { title: 'Description' },\
\]);

</script\>

### Vue Scheduler Tutorial

See the [Vue Scheduler: Row Header Templates](https://code.daypilot.org/46249/vue-scheduler-row-header-templates)
 tutorial that shows how to add icons, badges and interactive content to the Vue Scheduler row headers:

[![Vue Scheduler Row Header Templates Tutorial](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/yz3r6si3ojez3mlfxddkk35w6a/Vue+Scheduler+Row+Header+Templates+Tutorial.png "Vue Scheduler Row Header Templates Tutorial")](https://code.daypilot.org/46249/vue-scheduler-row-header-templates)

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

It's possible to change the following properties:

*   Areas ([row header active areas](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/row-header-active-areas/ "Row Header Active Areas")
    )
    
*   BackgroundColor
    
*   Columns (additional [row header columns](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/row-header-columns/ "Row Header Columns")
    )
    
*   CssClass
    
*   Html
    
*   ToolTip
    

### Background Color Example

protected void DayPilotScheduler1\_BeforeResHeaderRender(object sender, DayPilot.Web.Ui.Events.Scheduler.BeforeResHeaderRenderEventArgs e)
{
  if (e.Id == "E")
  {
    e.BackgroundColor = "green";
  }
}

### Custom CSS Example

protected void DayPilotScheduler1\_BeforeResHeaderRender(object sender, DayPilot.Web.Ui.Events.Scheduler.BeforeResHeaderRenderEventArgs e)
{
  if (e.Id == "E")
  {
    e.CssClass = "my-row";
  }
}

The custom CSS class will be applied to all row header cells (if there are multiple [row header columns](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/row-header-columns/ "Row Header Columns")
 defined).

### Custom HTML Example

You can use the e.Html property to customize the row header HTML (e.g. insert a hyperlink).

protected void DayPilotScheduler1\_BeforeResHeaderRender(object sender, DayPilot.Web.Ui.Events.Scheduler.BeforeResHeaderRenderEventArgs e)
{
  string encodedName = System.Web.HttpUtility.HtmlEncode(e.Name);
  e.Html = String.Format("<a href='Resource.aspx?id={0}'>{1}</a>", e.Id, encodedName);
}

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

protected override void OnBeforeResHeaderRender(BeforeResHeaderRenderArgs e)
{
  if (Id == "dps\_areas")
  {
      e.Areas.Add(new Area().Width(17).Bottom(1).Right(0).Top(0).CssClass("resource\_action\_menu").Html("<div><div></div></div>").JavaScript("alert(e.Value);"));
  }

  if (e.Columns.Count > 0)
  {
      e.Columns\[0\].Html = "10 seats";
  }
}

Upper-Left Corner
=================

![Upper Left Corner of JavaScript Scheduler](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/jwjlendbtng43jsltcnkapcdvu/Upper-Left+Corner+of+JavaScript+Scheduler.png "Upper Left Corner of JavaScript Scheduler")

You can customize the upper-left corner of the JavaScript Scheduler component using the `cornerText`/`cornerHtml` properties or using the `onBeforeCornerRender` event.

*   The [cornerText](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-cornertext/)
     value will be automatically encoded.
    
*   The [cornerHtml](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-cornerhtml/)
     value lets you set raw HTML.
    
*   The [onBeforeCornerRender](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-onbeforecornerrender/)
     event lets you override the content dynamically during every update.
    
*   To customize the corner content during [image export](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/client-side-export/)
    , use the [onBeforeCornerExport](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-onbeforecornerexport/)
     event.
    

Note that when using named [row header columns](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/row-header-columns/ "Row Header Columns")
, the space available in the upper-left corner will be smaller.

JavaScript Scheduler
--------------------

Initial value:

const scheduler = new DayPilot.Scheduler("scheduler", {
  cornerHtml: DayPilot.Date.today().getYear(),
  // ...
});
scheduler.init();

Force a new value (override the current `cornerHtml`):

scheduler.update({
  cornerHtml: DayPilot.Date.today().getYear(),
});

Dynamic value, evaluated on every update:

const scheduler = new DayPilot.Scheduler("scheduler", {
  onBeforeCornerRender: args => {
     args.html = DayPilot.Date.today().getYear(),
  },
  // ...
});
scheduler.init();

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

Initial page load/PostBack

DayPilotScheduler1.CornerHtml = String.Format("<div style\='padding:5px; font-weight: bold; font-size:22px; text-align:center'\>{0}</div\>", DayPilotScheduler1.StartDate.Year);

CallBack

DayPilotScheduler1.CornerHtml = String.Format("<div style\='padding:5px; font-weight: bold; font-size:22px; text-align:center'\>{0}</div\>", DayPilotScheduler1.StartDate.Year);
DayPilotScheduler1.Update();

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

CallBack

CornerHtml = String.Format("<div style\='padding:5px; font-weight: bold; font-size:22px; text-align:center'\>{0}</div\>", StartDate.Year);
Update();

Separators
==========

![html5 scheduler separators grid](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/ixu3dom3ljam3obxgrzp5mcd6a/html5-scheduler-separators-grid.png "html5 scheduler separators grid")

Separators are vertical lines inserted at specified time points.

You can define separators using the `separators` property.

### Appearance

You can control the appearance using the following properties:

*   `location` (time position)
    
*   `color` (color)
    
*   `width` (width in pixels)
    
*   `layer` (above or below the events)
    
*   for the full list, see the API docs for [DayPilot.Scheduler.separators](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-separators/)
    

JavaScript
----------

See [DayPilot.Scheduler.separators](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-separators/)
 array.

### Examples

Inserting a red separator at the specified date/time position:

<div id\="dp"\></div\>
<script type\="text/javascript"\>
  var dp = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dp");
  dps.separators =  \[{color:"red", location:"2019-03-29T00:00:00"}\];
  // ...
  dp.init();
</script\>

Highlighting the [current time](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/highlighting-current-time/ "Highlighting Current Time")
:

<div id\="dp"\></div\>
<script type\="text/javascript"\>
  var dp = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dp");
  dps.separators = \[{color:"red", location: new DayPilot.Date()}\];
  // ...
  dp.init();
</script\>

Separator with a custom width:

<div id\="dp"\></div\>
<script type\="text/javascript"\>
  var dp = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dp");
  dps.separators = \[{color:"red", location: new DayPilot.Date(), width: 5}\];
  // ...
  dp.init();
</script\>

Separator displayed above events:

<div id\="dp"\></div\>
<script type\="text/javascript"\>
  var dp = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dp");
  dps.separators = \[{color:"red", location: new DayPilot.Date(), layer: "AboveEvents" }\];
  // ...
  dp.init();
</script\>

### Tutorials

*   [JavaScript Scheduler: Show Separators in the Time Header](https://code.daypilot.org/97796/javascript-scheduler-show-separators-in-the-time-header)
    

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

ASPX

<DayPilot:DayPilotScheduler ID\="DayPilotScheduler1" runat\="server" ... >
  ...
  <Separators\>
    <DayPilot:Separator Color\="green" Location\="02/01/2019 12:00:00" />
    <DayPilot:Separator Color\="green" Location\="02/03/2019 12:00:00" />
    <DayPilot:Separator Color\="red" Location\="02/02/2019 00:00:00" />
  </Separators\>
</DayPilot:DayPilotScheduler\>

**Custom Separator**

![calendar separator 241x181](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/ktrcwrfnb5eirnzgsbztccs7eq/calendar-separator-241x181.png "calendar separator 241x181")

// Red separator with 50% opacity placed at the current day with width equal to CellWidth, shown above events
DayPilotScheduler1.Separators.Add(DateTime.Today, Color.Red, SeparatorLayer.AboveEvents, DayPilotScheduler1.CellWidth, 50);

![calendar separator 241x181](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/h6yjbpvcvbbrtkgyoemdmycbpu/calendar-separator-241x181.png "calendar separator 241x181")

Example above:

// Red separator with 50% opacity placed at the current day with width equal to CellWidth, shown above events
DayPilotScheduler1.Separators.Add(DateTime.Today, Color.Red, SeparatorLayer.AboveEvents, DayPilotScheduler1.CellWidth, 50);

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

protected override void OnInit(InitArgs ea)
{
  // ...
  Separators.Clear();
  Separators.Add(DateTime.Now, Color.Red);
}

Hiding Non-Business Hours
=========================

![javascript scheduler hiding non business hours](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/pmzdrbvi2zdn3lgykk3nq4uyq4/javascript-scheduler-hiding-non-business-hours.png "javascript scheduler hiding non business hours")

It is possible to hide cells outside of business hours (specified using `businessBeginsHour` and `businessEndsHour`) using `showNonBusiness` property.

You can also [hide selected time columns](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/hiding-time-columns/ "Hiding Time Columns")
 or [generate a custom timeline](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/timeline/ "Timeline")
.

JavaScript
----------

See also [DayPilot.Scheduler.onIncludeTimeCell](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-onincludetimecell/)
 and [hiding time columns](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/hiding-time-columns/ "Hiding Time Columns")
.

Hiding the predefined non-business hours:

<div id\="dp"\></div\>

<script\>
  var dp = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dp");
  dp.businessBeginsHour = 9;
  dp.businessEndsHour = 18;
  dp.showNonBusiness = false;
  // ...
  dp.init();

</script\>

Column hiding customization:

<div id\="dp"\></div\>
<script type\="text/javascript"\>
  var dp = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dp");
  dp.onIncludeTimeCell = function(args) {
    if (args.cell.start.getDayOfWeek() === 0 || args.cell.start.getDayOfWeek() === 6) { // hide Saturdays, Sundays
      args.cell.visible = false;
    }
  };
  // ...
  dp.init();
</script\>

### Demo

*   [JavaScript Scheduler: Hiding Non-Business Hours Demo](https://javascript.daypilot.org/demo/scheduler/hidingnonbusiness.html)
    

### Tutorial

*   [JavaScript Scheduler: Skip Non-Business Cells during Drag and Drop](https://code.daypilot.org/57279/javascript-scheduler-skip-non-business-cells-during-drag-an)
    

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

You can turn this feature on just by setting:

*   ShowNonBusiness="false"
    

This setup will hide:

*   Weekends (Saturday and Sunday)
    
*   Hours before BusinessBeginsHour (default value is 9) and after BusinessEndsHour (default 18)
    

**DayPilot Scheduler: Showing Workdays Only**

![asp.net scheduler hiding weekends](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/rkrt6mygvnetjhcjrfnozgoeoq/asp.net-scheduler-hiding-weekends.png "asp.net scheduler hiding weekends")

.aspx

<DayPilot:DayPilotScheduler
  ...
  Scale\="Day"
  ShowNonBusiness\="false"
/>

**DayPilot Scheduler: Showing 9AM - 5PM (+Lunch Break 12PM - 1PM**)

![asp.net scheduler hiding non business hours custom](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/ojw7i4wwp5dudefdmavdt3h26a/asp.net-scheduler-hiding-non-business-hours-custom.png "asp.net scheduler hiding non business hours custom")

.aspx

<DayPilot:DayPilotScheduler
  ...
  BusinessBeginsHour\="9"
  BusinessEndsHour\="18"
  ShowNonBusiness\="false"
  OnIncludeCell\="DayPilotScheduler1\_IncludeCell"
/>

.aspx.cs

protected void DayPilotScheduler1\_IncludeCell(object sender, IncludeCellEventArgs e)
{
  if (e.Start.Hour == 12)
  {
      e.Visible = false;
  }
}

**DayPilot Scheduler: Showing 9AM - 5PM (Lunch Break Highlighted)**

![asp.net scheduler hiding non business hours lunch break](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/rn6faugcbzgp7omzyvxdfobcum/asp.net-scheduler-hiding-non-business-hours-lunch-break.png "asp.net scheduler hiding non business hours lunch break")

Column hiding can be combined with [cell highlighting](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/cell-customization/ "Cell Customization")
. The lunch break can be **highlighted** instead using BeforeCellRender event handler.

.aspx

<DayPilot:DayPilotScheduler
  ...
  BusinessBeginsHour\="9"
  BusinessEndsHour\="18"
  ShowNonBusiness\="false"
  OnBeforeCellRender\="DayPilotScheduler1\_BeforeCellRender"
/>

.aspx.cs

protected void DayPilotScheduler1\_BeforeCellRender(object sender, DayPilot.Web.Ui.Events.BeforeCellRenderEventArgs e)
{
  if (e.Start.Hour == 12)
  {
    e.IsBusiness = false;
  }
}

Note that setting **IsBusiness** property in BeforeCellRender will not hide the column, but rather use the color specified for non-business hours (NonBusinessBackColor) for that cell.

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

![scheduler for asp.net mvc hiding weekends](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/7hrk5dwp45bwvjqnv5fxi2ta5i/scheduler-for-asp.net-mvc-hiding-weekends.png "scheduler for asp.net mvc hiding weekends")

It is possible to hide the non-business hours by setting **ShowNonBusiness = false**.

In this mode, weekends and all time columns defined as non-business using the **BusinessBeginsHour** and **BusinessEndsHour** range will be hidden.

See also [hiding time columns](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/hiding-time-columns/ "Hiding Time Columns")
.

Gantt Chart
===========

![javascript gantt chart view](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/bbsnbbix6rbmpktb6ux2vdtjiq/javascript-gantt-chart-view.png "javascript gantt chart view")

DayPilot Scheduler can display a **Gantt Chart** by simply switching the ViewType property to "Gantt".

**Note:** Since DayPilot Pro 7.9 this ViewType is superseded by a special [Gantt Chart component](https://doc.daypilot.org/gantt/ "Gantt Chart Component")
.

### How It Works

In the Gantt mode, the Scheduler will generate the [rows](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/rows/ "Rows")
 automatically from the event set:

*   Each event/task will be displayed on a separate row.
    
*   You can [customize the task box](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/event-customization/ "Event Customization")
     using BeforeEventRender event (HTML, CSS class, context menu, action restrictions, active hover areas, etc.)
    
*   You can [customize the row header](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/row-header-customization/ "Row Header Customization")
     using BeforeResHeaderRender event (HTML, CSS class, active hover areas)
    
*   Tasks can be [moved](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/event-moving/ "Event Moving")
     and [resized](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/event-resizing/ "Event Resizing")
     using drag and drop
    

### Drag and Drop

![gantt chart asp.net drag and drop](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/owet2w4q55fqldz7nmb7b4m2v4/gantt-chart-asp.net-drag-and-drop.png "gantt chart asp.net drag and drop")

The tasks can be  [moved](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/event-moving/ "Event Moving")
 and [resized](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/event-resizing/ "Event Resizing")
 right in place using drag and drop.

### Percent Complete

![gantt chart asp.net percent complete](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/g66vm6b36zey5pclgxxcpolcmu/gantt-chart-asp.net-percent-complete.png "gantt chart asp.net percent complete")

In order to use the duration bar to display **task progress** (as [percentage](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/percent-complete/ "Percent Complete")
), use DurationBarMode="PercentComplete" and set the value in BeforeEventRender event.

### Full CSS Styling

All elements can be styled using pure [CSS](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/css/ "CSS")
.

### CSS Themes

Examples of available [themes](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/css-themes/ "CSS Themes")
:

Green Theme

![gantt chart asp.net theme green](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/dgrln2ept5ad7flsnp5ep2i7qm/gantt-chart-asp.net-theme-green.png "gantt chart asp.net theme green")

White Theme

![gantt chart asp.net theme white](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/nt5gbiayibfwbfxprhmayk5h2q/gantt-chart-asp.net-theme-white.png "gantt chart asp.net theme white")

Transparent Theme

![gantt chart asp.net theme transparent](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/obatiofz6fcl3p5vlo7fa3dssm/gantt-chart-asp.net-theme-transparent.png "gantt chart asp.net theme transparent")

### CSS Theme Designer

[![gantt chart asp.net theme designer](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/jfl3ojvcezblfjilanbebbe4rm/gantt-chart-asp.net-theme-designer.png "gantt chart asp.net theme designer")](http://themes.daypilot.org/)

Create your own CSS theme in the online [theme designer](http://themes.daypilot.org/)
 application.

### Task Details

![gantt chart asp.net task detail](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/2xcylu33cfdajgni47w4lchuli/gantt-chart-asp.net-task-detail.png "gantt chart asp.net task detail")

One of the ways to display the event details is [task bubble](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/event-bubble/ "Event Bubble")
 - an extended HTML tooltip that is displayed on hover.

### Active Areas

You can define custom [active areas](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/event-active-areas/ "Event Active Areas")
 inside the task box that will be permanently **visible** or that will be displayed on **hover**. 

An active area can have an **action** associated to it:

*   context menu
    
*   drag handler for moving
    
*   drag handler for resizing
    
*   custom JavaScript action
    

**Event delete icon example**

![gantt chart asp.net area delete](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/4miug7itzbgc7ptcyzatbpgpga/gantt-chart-asp.net-area-delete.png "gantt chart asp.net area delete")

**Event context menu example**

![gantt chart asp.net area context menu](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/hybwkwvqwfepbmxnrxcivvmuly/gantt-chart-asp.net-area-context-menu.png "gantt chart asp.net area context menu")

JavaScript
----------

<div id\="dp"\></div\>
<script type\="text/javascript"\>
  var dp = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dp");
  dp.viewType = "Gantt";
  // ...
  dp.init();
</script\>

### Demo

*   [http://javascript.daypilot.org/demo/scheduler/gantt.html](http://javascript.daypilot.org/demo/scheduler/gantt.html)
    

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

<DayPilot:DayPilotScheduler
  ID\="DayPilotScheduler1" 
  runat\="server" 

  DataEndField\="TaskEnd"
  DataStartField\="TaskStart" 
  DataTextField\="TaskName" 
  DataValueField\="TaskId" 

  ViewType\="Gantt"
/>

For a **sample ASP.NET web project** that demonstrates how to display a Gantt chart using an SQL database, see the [Gantt Chart Tutorial](http://code.daypilot.org/68118/gantt-chart-tutorial-asp-net-sql-server-c-vb-net)
 \[code.daypilot.org\]. The download includes both C# and VB.NET versions.

### Demo

*   [Gantt Chart Demo](http://www.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/Gantt.aspx)
    

### Tutorial

*   [Gantt Chart Tutorial](http://code.daypilot.org/68118/gantt-chart-tutorial-asp-net-sql-server-c-vb-net)
     \[code.daypilot.org\]
    

### Example

protected void DayPilotScheduler1\_BeforeEventRender(object sender, BeforeEventRenderEventArgs e)
{
  int complete = (int) e.DataItem\["complete"\]; // use "complete" field of your DataSource items  
  e.PercentComplete = complete;  

  string cs = String.Format("{0}%", complete);  // override the task text
  e.InnerHTML = cs;
}

### Export as Image (PNG, GIF, JPG)

![gantt chart asp.net png export](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/rpeiyp3icvcxposrz3ewt6qgxq/gantt-chart-asp.net-png-export.png "gantt chart asp.net png export")

The Gantt chart can be exported as image.

*   Formats: PNG, JPG, GIF, BMP, TIFF ([MemoryStream](http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/system.io.memorystream.aspx)
     class)
    
*   Raw [Bitmap](http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/system.drawing.bitmap.aspx)
     class (you can further modify the image or merge it with other images)
    
*   Add images to PDF files, e-mails, or display static snapshots of the gantt chart
    

### Active Areas

protected void DayPilotScheduler1\_BeforeEventRender(object sender, BeforeEventRenderEventArgs e)
{
    e.Areas.Add(new Area().Width(17).Bottom(9).Right(19).Top(3).CssClass("event\_action\_delete").JavaScript("dps1.commandCallBack('delete', {id:e.value() });"));
    e.Areas.Add(new Area().Width(17).Bottom(9).Right(2).Top(3).CssClass("event\_action\_menu").ContextMenu("cmSpecial"));
}

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

@Html.DayPilotScheduler("dps", new DayPilotSchedulerConfig {
  BackendUrl = ResolveUrl("~/Scheduler/Backend"),
  ...
  ViewType = DayPilot.Web.Mvc.Enums.Scheduler.ViewType.Gantt
  })

*   [Gantt Chart for ASP.NET MVC Tutorial](http://code.daypilot.org/88253/gantt-chart-for-asp-net-mvc)
     (C#, VB.NET, SQL Server)

*   [Gantt Chart for ASP.NET MVC](http://mvc.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/Gantt)
     Demo

Time Header Rows
================

![javascript html5 scheduler time headers](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/egxwoby46jajff7iboxvyq3ayq/javascript-html5-scheduler-time-headers.png "javascript html5 scheduler time headers")

The scheduler time header rows can be customized using `timeHeaders` property.

1.  You have to specify the `groupBy` unit for each of the time header row (`"Hour"`, `"Day"`, `"Week"`, `"Month"`, `"Year"`, etc.). See also [time header groups](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/time-header-groups/ "Time Header Groups")
    .
    
2.  You can also customize the date format using `format` property (e.g. `"MMMM yyyy"`). For time format string pattern see [DayPilot.Date.toString()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-date-tostring/)
    .
    

The header cell HTML can be [customized using onBeforeTimeHeaderRender](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/time-header-customization/ "Time Header Customization")
 event.

### Default Time Header Rows

![javascript html5 scheduler time header rows default](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/44hof7fcafegvmcc6rmhsqetj4/javascript-html5-scheduler-time-header-rows-default.png "javascript html5 scheduler time header rows default")

The default time header levels are defined as follows:

*   0: `groupBy: "Default"` (the value defined using CellGroupBy property)
    
*   1: `groupBy: "Cell"` (corresponds to grid [cell duration](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/time-cell-duration/ "Time Cell Duration")
    )
    

### Supported GroupBy Values

See [time header groups](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/time-header-groups/ "Time Header Groups")
.

JavaScript
----------

You can set the time headers using [timeHeaders](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-timeheaders/)
 property:

dp.timeHeaders = \[ \
  {groupBy: 'Month', format: 'MMMM yyyy'}, \
  {groupBy: 'Week'},\
  {groupBy: 'Day'}\
\];

See also

*   [DayPilot.Scheduler.timeHeaders](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-timeheaders/)
     \[api.daypilot.org\]
    

Demo

*   [JavaScript Scheduler: Time Headers Demo](https://javascript.daypilot.org/demo/scheduler/timeheaders.html)
    

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

It's possible to show multiple rows of column groups using `TimeHeaders` collection.

![scheduler time headers multi row](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/h5axwhv4f5hjbl3cpfxg4i2kju/scheduler-time-headers-multi-row.png "scheduler time headers multi row")

**Example (.aspx)**

<DayPilot:DayPilotScheduler runat\="server" ID\="DayPilotScheduler1"  ... >
  <TimeHeaders\>
    <DayPilot:TimeHeader GroupBy\="Month" Format\="MMMM yyyy" />
    <DayPilot:TimeHeader GroupBy\="Week" />
    <DayPilot:TimeHeader GroupBy\="Day" />
  </TimeHeaders\>
</DayPilot:DayPilotScheduler\>

**Example (C#)**

protected void Page\_Load(object sender, EventArgs e)
{
  // ...
	
  DayPilotScheduler1.TimeHeaders.Clear();
  DayPilotScheduler1.TimeHeaders.Add(new TimeHeader(GroupByEnum.Month, "MMMM yyyy"));
  DayPilotScheduler1.TimeHeaders.Add(new TimeHeader(GroupByEnum.Week));
  DayPilotScheduler1.TimeHeaders.Add(new TimeHeader(GroupByEnum.Day));
  // ...
}

**Demo**

*   [Time Header Scheduler Demo](http://www.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/TimeHeaders.aspx)
    

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

You can display multiple levels of time headers grouped by different units using `TimeHeaders` property

![scheduler asp.net mvc time header levels](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/d4ogg626sjfancg7brqgjyfmbi/scheduler-asp.net-mvc-time-header-levels.png "scheduler asp.net mvc time header levels")

@Html.DayPilotScheduler("dps\_timeheaders", new DayPilotSchedulerConfig {
  BackendUrl = ResolveUrl("~/Scheduler/Backend"),
  ...
  CellDuration = 1440,
  TimeHeaders = new TimeHeaderCollection {
    new TimeHeader(GroupBy.Month, "MMMM yyyy"),
    new TimeHeader(GroupBy.Week),
    new TimeHeader(GroupBy.Day)
  }
})

### Demo

*   [http://mvc.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/TimeHeaders](http://mvc.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/TimeHeaders)
    

Availability
------------

Availability of this feature in DayPilot editions:

Header Date and Time Format
===========================

DayPilot Scheduler uses predefined DateTime formats depending on the time unit used in the header:

Row Header Groups
-----------------

### GroupBy="Hour"

*   Hour in format determined by TimeFormat property (12 or 24 hour clock).
    

Example: `"12 AM"`

### GroupBy="Day"

*   Date formatted using DateTime.ToLongDateString().
    

Example: `"Sunday, January 25, 2039"`

### GroupBy="Week" 

*   Week number according to ISO 8601
    

### GroupBy="Month"

*   Month formatted using DateTime.ToString("MMMM yyyy").
    

Example: `"January 2009"`

### GroupBy="None"

*   Empty string.
    

Custom format
-------------

See [Time Header Customization](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/time-header-customization/ "Time Header Customization")
.

First Day of Week
=================

The first day of week can be set using the _weekStarts_ property.

### Notes

*   The _weekStarts_ property only affects the [time header groups](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/time-header-groups/ "Time Header Groups")
    .
*   The **week numbers** are calculated according to [ISO 8601](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)
     (assumes Monday is the first day of week).

JavaScript
----------

Possible values:

*   \- Sunday
*   _1_\- Monday
*   _2_ - Tuesday
*   _3_ - Wednesday
*   _4_ - Thursday
*   _5_ - Friday
*   _6_ - Saturday
*   _"Auto"_ (default)

<div id\="dp"\></div\>
<script type\="text/javascript"\>
  var dp = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dp");
  dp.weekStarts = 0;
  // ...
  dp.init();
</script\>

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

Possible values:

*   Monday
*   Tuesday
*   Wednesday
*   Thursday
*   Friday
*   Saturday
*   Sunday
*   **Auto (default)**

The automatic mode uses the current culture to determine the first day of week.

<DayPilot:DayPilotScheduler runat\="server" id\="DayPilotScheduler1"
  ...
  WeekStarts = "Sunday"
/>

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

Possible values:

*   Monday
*   Tuesday
*   Wednesday
*   Thursday
*   Friday
*   Saturday
*   Sunday
*   **Auto (default)**

The automatic mode uses the current culture to determine the first day of week.

@Html.DayPilotScheduler("dps", new DayPilotSchedulerConfig {
  BackendUrl = ResolveUrl("~/Scheduler/Backend"),
  ...
  WeekStarts = WeekStarts.Sunday
})
