        *   [View Types](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/view-types/ "View Types")
            
        *   [XSS Protection](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/xss-protection/ "XSS Protection")
            

CommandCallBack()
=================

The commandCallBack() method can be used to send custom command with custom data to the server side.

### Typical Use

*   Reload the event data
*   Switch the visible time range
*   Refresh the control after changing the resources
*   Filtering the visible events

### How It Works

When you invoke the _commandCallBack()_ function on the client-side DayPilot Scheduler object, it will fire the _Command_event handler on the server-side using an AJAX call.

In the _Command_ event handler you should call _Update()_ after doing your work and reloading the events using _DataBind()_.

See also: [**Update() method**](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/callback-update/ "CallBack Update")

### Parameters

*   command (string) - custom command type string
*   data (any JavaScript object) - custom data that will be sent to the server-side Command handler

The data parameter will be translated into JsonData class - see also **[Sending custom data with CallBack](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/callback-with-custom-data/ "CallBack with Custom Data")
**.

### Reference

*   [DayPilot.Scheduler.commandCallBack()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-commandcallback/)
    

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

Example (switching to a specified year)

.aspx

<a href="javascript:dps1.commandCallBack('year', 2013);">Next</a>

.aspx.cs:

    protected void DayPilotScheduler1\_Command(object sender, DayPilot.Web.Ui.Events.CommandEventArgs e)
    {
        switch (e.Command)
        {
            case "year":
                DayPilotScheduler1.StartDate = new DateTime((int)e.Data, 1, 1);
                DayPilotScheduler1.Days = Year.Days(DayPilotScheduler1.StartDate.Year);
                break;
            // ...
        }
        // ...
        DayPilotScheduler1.DataBind();
        DayPilotScheduler1.Update(CallBackUpdateType.Full);
    }

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

View

<a href="javascript:dps.commandCallBack('next');">Next</a>
<a href="javascript:dps.commandCallBack('previous');">Previous</a>

<a href="javascript:dps.commandCallBack('delete', { id: 123});">Delete Event with ID=123</a>

Controller (SchedulerController.cs)

class Dps : DayPilotScheduler
{

  protected override void OnCommand(CommandArgs e)
  {
      switch (e.Command)
      {
          case "refresh":
              UpdateWithMessage("Refreshed");
              break;
          case "next":
              StartDate = StartDate.AddYears(1);
              Days = Year.Days(StartDate);
              Update(CallBackUpdateType.Full);
              break;
          case "previous":
              StartDate = StartDate.AddYears(-1);
              Days = Year.Days(StartDate);
              Update(CallBackUpdateType.Full);
              break;
          case "this":
              StartDate = Year.First(DateTime.Today);
              Days = Year.Days(StartDate);
              Update(CallBackUpdateType.Full);
              break;
          case "delete":
              string id = (string)e.Data\["id"\];
              new EventManager(Controller).EventDelete(id);
              Update(CallBackUpdateType.EventsOnly);
              break;
      }
  }
}

*   [Touch Devices](http://doc.daypilot.org/month/touch-devices/ "Touch Devices")
    
*   [View Types](http://doc.daypilot.org/month/view-types/ "View Types")
    

This **monthly event calendar widget** is available for pure [JavaScript](http://javascript.daypilot.org/month/)
, [jQuery](http://doc.daypilot.org/month/jquery/ "jQuery")
, [PHP](http://code.daypilot.org/57519/monthly-event-calendar-for-javascript-html5-php)
, [ASP.NET WebForms](http://www.daypilot.org/month.html)
, [ASP.NET MVC](http://mvc.daypilot.org/month/)
, [Java](http://java.daypilot.org/month/)
 (J2EE/JSP).

Business Days
=============

![monthly event calendar asp.net business](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/ns26fvpj45eevnzatfhb2xuoue/monthly-event-calendar-asp.net-business.png "monthly event calendar asp.net business")

Business cells (defined as Monday-Friday) are now marked with **\_cell\_business** class in [CssOnly mode](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/cssonly-mode/ "CssOnly Mode")
.

Styling business cells in now supported in the [theme designer](http://themes.daypilot.org/)
 as well.

See also:

*   [http://kb.daypilot.org/77032/list-of-css-classes-used-in-cssonly-mode-month/](http://kb.daypilot.org/77032/list-of-css-classes-used-in-cssonly-mode-month/)

# Bubble | DayPilot Documentation - Scheduling Components for
    JavaScript, Angular, React and Vue

[![DayPilot](https://static.daypilot.org/img/logo/daypilot-logo-40x40.png)DayPilot](https://www.daypilot.org/)

[Products](https://www.daypilot.org/products/)

[Support](https://www.daypilot.org/support/)

  

[DayPilot Documentation](https://doc.daypilot.org/)

*   [Home](https://doc.daypilot.org/)
    *   [Bubble](https://doc.daypilot.org/bubble/ "Bubble")
        *   [Activation](https://doc.daypilot.org/bubble/activation/ "Activation")
            
        *   [Animation](https://doc.daypilot.org/bubble/animation/ "Animation")
            
        *   [CSS](https://doc.daypilot.org/bubble/css/ "CSS")
            
        *   [Hide Timeout](https://doc.daypilot.org/bubble/hide-timeout/ "Hide Timeout")
            
        *   [Loading Label](https://doc.daypilot.org/bubble/loading-label/ "Loading Label")
            
        *   [Position](https://doc.daypilot.org/bubble/position/ "Position")
            
        *   [Source Objects](https://doc.daypilot.org/bubble/source-objects/ "Source Objects")
            
        *   [Width](https://doc.daypilot.org/bubble/width/ "Width")
            

Bubble
======

![javascript scheduler bubble popover](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/gs7qeiy3g5e6dl6r4xsppwmqxi/javascript-scheduler-bubble-popover.png "javascript scheduler bubble popover")

*   [Activation](http://doc.daypilot.org/bubble/activation/ "Activation")
    
*   [Animation](http://doc.daypilot.org/bubble/animation/ "Animation")
    
*   [CSS](http://doc.daypilot.org/bubble/css/ "CSS")
    
*   [Hide Timeout](http://doc.daypilot.org/bubble/hide-timeout/ "Hide Timeout")
    
*   [Loading Label](http://doc.daypilot.org/bubble/loading-label/ "Loading Label")
    
*   [Position](http://doc.daypilot.org/bubble/position/ "Position")
    
*   [Source Objects](http://doc.daypilot.org/bubble/source-objects/ "Source Objects")
    
*   [Width](http://doc.daypilot.org/bubble/width/ "Width")
    

JavaScript
----------

### Example: Pre-Loaded HTML

The bubble HTML can be specified as part of the event data (bubbleHtml property).

<script type="text/javascript">
    var dp = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dp");

    // ...

    dp.showToolTip = false;
    dp.bubble = new DayPilot.Bubble();

    // ...

    var e = new DayPilot.Event({
      start: new DayPilot.Date("2013-03-25T00:00:00").addHours(start),
      end: new DayPilot.Date("2013-03-25T12:00:00").addHours(start).addHours(duration),
      id: DayPilot.guid(),
      resource: "B",
      text: "Event",
      bubbleHtml: "Testing bubble"
    });
    dp.events.add(e); 

</script>    

### Example: Dynamic HTML

    dp.bubble = new DayPilot.Bubble({
        onLoad: function(args) {
            var ev = args.source;
            args.html = "testing bubble for: " + ev.text();
        }
    });

### Example: Asynchronous Loading

    dp.bubble = new DayPilot.Bubble({
        onLoad: function(args) {
            var ev = args.source;
            args.async = true;  // manual notification using .loaded() is required, see below
            
            // simulating slow server-side load
            setTimeout(function() {
                args.html = "testing bubble for: <br>" + ev.text();
                args.loaded();
            }, 500);
        }
    });

    JavaScript, Angular, React and Vue

*   [Home](https://doc.daypilot.org/)
    *   [Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/ "Scheduler Component")
        *   [Appearance](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/appearance/ "Appearance")

CSS Themes
==========

![scheduler component css theme javascript angular react vue](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/biprtnhwrnfmzbmmv2cgbutq2m/scheduler-component-css-theme-javascript-angular-react-vue.png "scheduler component css theme javascript angular react vue")

The [JavaScript Scheduler](https://javascript.daypilot.org/scheduler/)
 component can be fully styled using **CSS themes**.

DayPilot includes a built-in theme (`"scheduler_default"`) that is embedded in the library. It is loaded automatically - you don't have to add any external CSS file.

To apply a custom theme to the Scheduler component, two steps are required:

*   Include the theme CSS in the page (usually it will be a standalone CSS file).
    
*   Set the theme to be used using the `theme` configuration property of the Scheduler.
    

DayPilot includes a couple of sample CSS themes (see the `/demo/themes` directory in the download package).

You can create your own theme easily using the online WYSIWYG [CSS Theme Designer](http://themes.daypilot.org/)
. The Theme Designer lets you customize the appearance of the key Scheduler elements, preview the changes immediately using a live instance of the Scheduler and download the CSS theme in a zip file.

### See Also

*   [Scheduler CSS classes](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/css-classes/ "CSS Classes")
    

JavaScript Scheduler
--------------------

In JavaScript, you can apply a custom CSS theme using the [theme](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-theme/)
 property.

Example

    <html>
    
    <head>
      <link type="text/css" rel="stylesheet" href="themes/scheduler_8.css"/>
    
      <!-- ... -->
    </head>
    <body>
    
      <div id="dp"></div>
      <script>
    
      const dp = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dp", {
        theme: "scheduler_8",
        // ...
      });
      dp.init();
    
      <script>
    </body>
    </html>

Angular Scheduler
-----------------

In the [Angular Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/angular/ "Angular")
 component, you can set the CSS theme using the `theme` property of the configuration object (`config` in the example below).

You can also change the CSS theme dynamically by changing `this.config.theme` anytime later. Angular will detect the change an update the Scheduler automatically.

It is necessary to include the theme CSS file in the HTML file. The best way to add the CSS theme to the application is to use an `@import` statement in `src/styles.css`.

CSS

    @import url('themes/scheduler_8.css');

TypeScript

    import {Component, ViewChild, AfterViewInit} from '@angular/core';
    import {DayPilot, DayPilotSchedulerComponent} from 'daypilot-pro-angular';
    import {DataService} from './data.service'; {}
    
    @Component({
      selector: 'scheduler-component',
      template: `
        <daypilot-scheduler [config]="config" [events]="events" #scheduler></daypilot-scheduler>
      `,
      styles: [``]
    })
    export class SchedulerComponent implements AfterViewInit {
    
      @ViewChild('scheduler')
      scheduler!: DayPilotSchedulerComponent;
    
      events: any[] = [];
    
      config: DayPilot.SchedulerConfig = {
        theme: "scheduler_8",
        // ...
      };
    
    }
    

Tutorial:

*   [Angular Scheduler: CSS Themes](https://code.daypilot.org/81784/angular-scheduler-css-themes)
    

React Scheduler
---------------

You can set the CSS theme of  the [React Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/react/ "React")
 component using the `theme` attribute of the `<DayPilotScheduler>` tag in JSX.

The CSS file with the theme definition must be included in the React application. The following example adds the `scheduler_white.css` theme using an `@import` statement in `src/index.css` file.

Example:

CSS

    @import url('themes/scheduler_white.css');

JSX:

    <DayPilotScheduler
      theme={"scheduler_white"}
    />
    

Tutorial:

*   [How to Use CSS Themes with the React Scheduler Component](https://code.daypilot.org/75128/how-to-use-css-themes-with-the-react-scheduler-component)
    

Vue Scheduler
-------------

The [Vue Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/vue-js/ "Vue")
 component supports definition of a custom CSS them using the `theme` property of the `config` object.

Also, include the theme CSS file in the application (this example uses `@import` in the `<style>` section of the Vue component).

    <template>
      <DayPilotScheduler id="dp" :config="config" ref="scheduler" />
    </template>
    
    <script>
    import {DayPilot, DayPilotScheduler} from 'daypilot-pro-vue'
    
    export default {
      name: 'Scheduler',
      data: function() {
        return {
          config: {
            theme: "scheduler_green",
          },
        }
      },
      components: {
        DayPilotScheduler
      },
      
      // ...
      
    }
    </script>
    
    <style>
      @import './assets/themes/scheduler_green.css';
    </style>
    

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

Set the theme using **Theme** property.

    Theme="scheduler_8"

Sample [Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/ "Scheduler Component")
 Themes:

*   [White](http://www.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/ThemeWhite.aspx)
    
*   [Blue](http://www.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/ThemeBlue.aspx)
    
*   [Green](http://www.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/ThemeGreen.aspx)
    
*   [Transparent](http://www.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/ThemeTransparent.aspx)
    
*   [Windows 8](http://www.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/Theme8.aspx)
    

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

Several sample themes are available:

*   [Windows 8](http://mvc.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/Theme8)
    
*   [Transparent](http://mvc.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/ThemeTransparent)
    
*   [Green](http://mvc.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/ThemeGreen)
    
*   [White](http://mvc.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/ThemeWhite)
    
*   [Blue](http://mvc.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/ThemeBlue)
    

Specify the scheduler theme name using **Theme** property:

    @Html.DayPilotScheduler("dps", new DayPilotSchedulerConfig {
            BackendUrl = ResolveUrl("~/Scheduler/Backend"),
            Theme = "scheduler_green",
            ...
        })

| [DayPilot for ASP.NET WebForms](https://www.daypilot.org/) |     |     |

Context Menu
============

![JavaScript Calendar Scheduler Context Menu](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/se7msv4nnjc7fomnaxixsjo544/JavaScript+Calendar+Scheduler+Context+Menu.png "JavaScript Calendar Scheduler Context Menu")

Business Hours
==============

![event calendar asp.net business](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/s4q467y6arhitawqgcbs26wozm/event-calendar-asp.net-business.png "event calendar asp.net business")

In the [JavaScript Calendar](https://javascript.daypilot.org/calendar/)
, you can specify the business hours range using the following properties:

*   [businessBeginsHour](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-calendar-businessbeginshour/)
    
*   [businessEndsHour](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-calendar-businessendshour/)
    

The business hours settings affect the following behavior:

*   The calendar control [height](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/height/ "Height")
     when the [heightSpec](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-calendar-heightspec/)
     property is set to `"BusinessHours"` or `"BusinessHoursNoScroll"`
    
*   Background cell color
    

The background cell color can be overridden using the [onBeforeCellRender](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/cell-customization/ "Cell Customization")
 event handler.

To hide the non-business hours, you can specify the displayed hour range using [dayBeginsHour](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-calendar-daybeginshour/)
 and [dayEndsHour](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-calendar-dayendshour/)
 properties (see also [overnight scheduling](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/overnight-scheduling/ "Overnight Scheduling")
).

### Weekends

You can show/hide weekends by selecting the ViewType:

*   ["WorkWeek"](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/work-week-view/ "Work Week View")
     (hides weekend days)
    
*   ["Week"](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/week-view/ "Week View")
     (shows weekends days)
    

### CSS

*   Business cells (defined as Monday-Friday, between BusinessBeginsHour and BusinessEndsHour) are now marked with**\_cell\_business** class in CssOnly mode.
    
*   Styling business cells in now supported in the [theme designer](http://themes.daypilot.org/)
     as well.
    

See also:

*   [http://kb.daypilot.org/31070/list-of-css-classes-used-in-cssonly-mode-calendar/](http://kb.daypilot.org/31070/list-of-css-classes-used-in-cssonly-mode-calendar/)
    

JavaScript
----------

Example

    onBeforeCellRender: (args) ==> {
        if (args.cell.start.getDay() === 0 || args.cell.start.getDay() === 0) {
            args.cell.business = true;
        }
    };

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

![calendar business hours](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/f4qcqm6idbhjzndixfancxlyzq/calendar-business-hours.png "calendar business hours")

You can define custom time cell colors using **BeforeCellRender** event:

    protected void DayPilotCalendar1_BeforeCellRender(object sender, BeforeCellRenderEventArgs e)
        {
            if (e.Start.Hour >= 9 && e.Start.Hour < 12)
                e.BackgroundColor = "#FFF2CC"; // shift #1 
            else if (e.Start.Hour >= 12 && e.Start.Hour < 15)
                e.BackgroundColor = "#FFD9CC"; // shift #2
            else if (e.Start.Hour >= 15 && e.Start.Hour < 18)
                e.BackgroundColor = "#F2FFCC"; // shift #3
        }

Turning Saturday and Sunday into business days:

        protected void DayPilotCalendar1_BeforeCellRender(object sender, BeforeCellRenderEventArgs e)
        {
            if ((e.Start.DayOfWeek == DayOfWeek.Saturday || e.Start.DayOfWeek == DayOfWeek.Sunday)
                || (e.Start.Hour >= 9 && e.Start.Hour < 18))
            {
                e.IsBusiness = true;
            }
       }

You can also change just the IsBusiness property and you will get the color automatically:

        protected void DayPilotCalendar1_BeforeCellRender(object sender, BeforeCellRenderEventArgs e)
        {
            if (e.Start.Hour >= 10 && e.Start.Hour < 12)
                e.IsBusiness = true;
            else if (e.Start.Hour >= 14 && e.Start.Hour < 16)
                e.IsBusiness = true;
            else
                e.IsBusiness = false;
        }

### See Also

*   [How to Define Calendar Business and Non-Business **Days**](http://kb.daypilot.org/39709/how-to-define-calendar-business-and-non-business-days/)
    
*   [How to show precise time in the Calendar row headers](http://kb.daypilot.org/75335/how-to-show-precise-time-in-the-calendar-row-headers/)
    
*   [Shift Scheduling Tutorial](http://code.daypilot.org/34377/shift-scheduling-tutorial-asp-net-sql-server-c-vb-net)
     with C#/VB.NET Source Code 

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

Turning Saturday and Sunday into business days:

    protected override void OnBeforeCellRender(BeforeCellRenderArgs e)
    {
      if ((e.Start.DayOfWeek == DayOfWeek.Saturday || e.Start.DayOfWeek == DayOfWeek.Sunday)
          || (e.Start.Hour >= 9 && e.Start.Hour < 18))
      {
          e.IsBusiness = true;
      }
    }

Context Menu
============

[Context menu](https://doc.daypilot.org/menu/ "Context Menu")
 is supported for following elements:

*   [event context menu](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/event-context-menu/ "Event Context Menu")
    
*   [time range context menu](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/time-range-context-menu/ "Time Range Context Menu")
    
*   [row header context menu](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/row-header-context-menu/ "Row Header Context Menu")
    

It can be invoked using [active area](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/active-areas/ "Active Areas")
.

        *   [Appearance](https://doc.daypilot.org/navigator/appearance/ "Appearance")
            *   [Orientation](https://doc.daypilot.org/navigator/orientation/ "Date Picker Orientation")
                
            *   [Rows per Month](https://doc.daypilot.org/navigator/rows-per-month/ "Rows per Month")

        *   [Frameworks](https://doc.daypilot.org/navigator/frameworks/ "Frameworks")

        *   [Navigation](https://doc.daypilot.org/navigator/navigation/ "Navigation")
            

Date Picker Orientation
=======================

You can set the date picker component orientation using the _orientation_ property.

*   The orientation affects how the month are arranged. It only applies when the date picker is configured to display [multiple months](https://doc.daypilot.org/navigator/months/ "Number of Months")
    .
*   The default orientation is _"Vertical"_.

### Horizontal

![navigator date picker horizontal orientation](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/55chckjyljbbld2tmebsgesltm/navigator-date-picker-horizontal-orientation.png "navigator date picker horizontal orientation")

### Vertical

![navigator date picker vertical orientation](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/mdnjy24l6bce7m4xdja2y5stbm/navigator-date-picker-vertical-orientation.png "navigator date picker vertical orientation")

### JavaScript Example

In the JavaScript date picker component, use the [orientation](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-navigator-orientation/)
 property:

<div id="nav"></div>

<script>

  const nav = new DayPilot.Navigator("nav", {
    // ...
    orientation: "Horizontal",
    showMonths: 3
  });

  nav.init();

</script>

Active Areas
============

![scheduler active areas event](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/3bmscsmi4verhcqllgpwtrzvqa/scheduler-active-areas-event.png "scheduler active areas event")

Active areas can be used to extend the event functionality and to add visual hints to the event.

*   You can define the dimension and position relative to the event box.
*   Customize the active areas using custom HTML and styles.

### Supported elements

*   [event active areas](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/event-active-areas/ "Event Active Areas")
     (see for event-specific examples)
*   [row header active areas](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/row-header-active-areas/ "Row Header Active Areas")
     (see for header-specific examples)

### Actions

*   open a context menu
*   display a bubble (through custom JavaScript)
*   [delete an event](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/event-deleting/ "Event Deleting")
     (through custom JavaScript)
*   move handler (events only)
*   resize handler (events only)
*   run custom JavaScript

# Navigator - Date Picker | DayPilot Documentation - Scheduling Components for
    JavaScript, Angular, React and Vue

[![DayPilot](https://static.daypilot.org/img/logo/daypilot-logo-40x40.png)DayPilot](https://www.daypilot.org/)

[Products](https://www.daypilot.org/products/)

[Support](https://www.daypilot.org/support/)

  

[DayPilot Documentation](https://doc.daypilot.org/)

*   [Home](https://doc.daypilot.org/)
    *   [Navigator](https://doc.daypilot.org/navigator/ "Navigator - Date Picker")
        *   [Appearance](https://doc.daypilot.org/navigator/appearance/ "Appearance")

        *   [Navigation](https://doc.daypilot.org/navigator/navigation/ "Navigation")
            

Navigator - Date Picker
=======================

![navigator date picker component](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/ii5sfo4y4zeznkexc7j632o7ge/navigator-date-picker-component.png "navigator date picker component")

**DayPilot Navigator** is a date picker component that you can use to select a day, week, or month. You can also select a custom date range using drag and drop.

The date picker can be combined with the main calendar component ([daily/weekly calendar](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/ "Event Calendar Component")
, [monthly calendar](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/ "Monthly Event Calendar Component")
, [resource scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/ "Scheduler Component")
) to allow changing the current calendar view.

*   [Appearance](http://doc.daypilot.org/navigator/appearance/ "Appearance")

*   [Days](https://doc.daypilot.org/navigator/days/ "Days")
    
*   [Events](http://doc.daypilot.org/navigator/events/ "Events")
    
*   [Localization](http://doc.daypilot.org/navigator/localization/ "Localization")
    
*   [Navigation](http://doc.daypilot.org/navigator/navigation/ "Navigation")

Progressive Event Rendering
===========================

![JavaScript Scheduler   Progressive Event Rendering](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/onbllxlugncxhe6zam3x5ofiza/JavaScript+Scheduler+-+Progressive+Event+Rendering.png "JavaScript Scheduler   Progressive Event Rendering")

With progressive event rendering enabled, the Scheduler events are not rendered at once but **progressively as a user scrolls**. This mode assumes all events are already available on the client side (compare with [dynamic event loading](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/dynamic-event-loading/ "Dynamic Event Loading")
).

The progressive rendering is enabled by default. You can turn it off using [dynamicEventRendering](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-dynamiceventrendering/)
 property.

In both the progressive and static modes the events are rendered in batches to provide visual feedback and prevent browser freezing.

### Viewport Size

The [dynamicEventRenderingMargin](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-dynamiceventrenderingmargin/)
 property will determine how many pixels will be loaded in addition to the current viewport. You can specify different margin for horizontal (X) and vertical (Y) axis:

*   [dynamicEventRenderingMarginX](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-dynamiceventrenderingmarginx/)
    
*   [dynamicEventRenderingMarginY](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-dynamiceventrenderingmarginy/)
    

### Sweeping

By default, the events that were rendered stay visible. This will gradually increase the DOM size and slow down the scrolling.

It's possible to **enable automatic sweeping** and specify the buffer size:

*   [dynamicEventRenderingCacheSize](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-dynamiceventrenderingcachesize/)
    
*   [dynamicEventRenderingCacheSweeping](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-dynamiceventrenderingcachesweeping/)
    

### Dynamic Loading

The progressive event rendering improves performance but it's necessary to preload the events. That will only work well up to a certain threshold. On fast machines, the Scheduler can display 100,000s of events (see the [Angular Scheduler demo that displays 400,000 events](https://code.daypilot.org/86168/angular-scheduler-loading-400000-events)
). However, these events need to be loaded from the server at once and the data set size can be quite big (400,000 events take about 46 MB uncompressed).

For larger data sets (> 1000 events), you may want to switch to [dynamic event loading](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/dynamic-event-loading/ "Dynamic Event Loading")
 which allows reading the data from the server in smaller chunks during scrolling.

### See Also

*   [Scrolling Performance](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/scrolling-performance/ "Scrolling Performance")
    

JavaScript
----------

    <div id="dp"></div>
    <script type="text/javascript">
      var dp = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dp");
      dp.dynamicEventRendering = "Disabled";
      // ...
      dp.init();
    </script>

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

    <DayPilot:DayPilotScheduler runat="server" id="DayPilotScheduler1"
      ...
      DynamicEventRendering = "Disabled"
    />

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

    @Html.DayPilotScheduler("dps", new DayPilotSchedulerConfig {
      BackendUrl = ResolveUrl("~/Scheduler/Backend"),
      ...
      DynamicEventRendering = DynamicEventRenderingType.Disabled
    })

Timesheet
=========

The [scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/ "Scheduler Component")
 can be switched to a **timesheet** mode:

*   The vertical (Y) axis displays **days**. The number of days is configurable using Days property. It can be a single day, a week, a month or a longer period.
    
*   The [height](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/height/ "Height")
     is controlled using HeightSpec property. You can display the timesheet in full height (all days) or limit it to the maximum number of pixels. A vertical scrollbar will be added if necessary.
    
*   The horizontal (X) axis displays 24 **hours of day**. Business hours are highlighted by default. The default business hours are defined as 9am - 5pm. You can customize them using BusinessBeginsHour and BusinessEndsHour properties.
    
*   You can apply the standard CSS [themes](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/css-themes/ "CSS Themes")
     created for the [scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/ "Scheduler Component")
    .
    
*   You can display additional [columns](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/row-header-columns/ "Row Header Columns")
     in the row header. This is useful for displaying daily summary.
    

JavaScript Timesheet
--------------------

![javascript html5 timesheet component](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/hdipak6v5vcx7n3gkvahjp44e4/javascript-html5-timesheet-component.png "javascript html5 timesheet component")

Here is an example configuration of the [JavaScript Scheduler](https://javascript.daypilot.org/scheduler/)
 that displays a timesheet UI:

    <div id="dp"></div>
    <script type="text/javascript">
      var dp = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dp");
      dp.viewType = "Days";
      dp.startDate = new DayPilot.Date().firstDayOfMonth();
      dp.days = dp.startDate.daysInMonth();
      /// ...
      dp.init();
    </script>

### JavaScript Tutorials

*   [HTML5 Timesheet (JavaScript, PHP)](http://code.daypilot.org/46581/html5-timesheet-javascript-php)
    
*   [ASP.NET Core Timesheet](https://code.daypilot.org/65371/asp-net-core-timesheet)
    

Angular Timesheet
-----------------

![angular timesheet quick start project](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/co2aqbo375fcrp5l3c6vflauna/angular-timesheet-quick-start-project.png "angular timesheet quick start project")

The [Angular Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/angular/ "Angular")
 component can display a timesheet if you switch the view type to `"Days"`.

    import {Component, ViewChild, AfterViewInit} from '@angular/core';
    import {DayPilot, DayPilotSchedulerComponent} from 'daypilot-pro-angular';
    
    @Component({
      selector: 'timesheet-component',
      template: `<daypilot-scheduler [config]="config" [events]="events" #timesheet></daypilot-scheduler>`,
      styles: [``]
    })
    export class TimesheetComponent {
    
      @ViewChild('timesheet')
      timesheet!: DayPilotSchedulerComponent;
    
      events: DayPilot.EventData[] = [];
    
      config: DayPilot.SchedulerConfig = {
        viewType: 'Days',
        // ...
      };
    
    }

### Angular Tutorials

*   [Angular 14 Timesheet Quick Start Project](https://code.daypilot.org/64510/angular-timesheet-quick-start-project)
    

React Timesheet
---------------

![react timesheet](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/fyt2j7gbdfhxvby4unuau3om3a/react-timesheet.png "react timesheet")

In the [React Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/react/ "React")
, you can activate the timesheet mode using `viewType` attribute of the `<DayPilotScheduler>` JSX tag:

    import React, {Component} from 'react';
    import {DayPilot, DayPilotScheduler} from "daypilot-pro-react";
    
    class Timesheet extends Component {
    
      render() {
        return (
          <div>
            <DayPilotScheduler
              viewType={"Days"}
            />
          </div>
        );
      }
    }
    
    export default Timesheet;

### React Tutorials

*   [React Timesheet Tutorial](https://code.daypilot.org/66143/react-timesheet)
    

Vue Timesheet
-------------

![vue timesheet](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/qsej4d4bznfdjges7qxgvdta2u/vue-timesheet.png "vue timesheet")

The [Vue Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/vue-js/ "Vue")
 component can display a timesheet using the following configuration:

    <template>
      <DayPilotScheduler id="dp" :config="config" />
    </template>
    
    <script>
    import {DayPilot, DayPilotScheduler} from 'daypilot-pro-vue'
    
    export default {
      name: 'Timesheet',
      data: function() {
        return {
          config: {
            viewType: "Days",
            timeHeaders: [{groupBy: "Hour"},{groupBy:"Cell", format:"mm"}],
            scale: "CellDuration",
            cellDuration: 15,
            viewType: "Days",
            startDate: "2021-10-01",
            days: 31,
            // ...
          },
        }
      },
      components: {
        DayPilotScheduler
      },
    }
    </script>
    
    

### Vue Tutorials

*   [Vue Timesheet Tutorial](https://code.daypilot.org/56186/vue-timesheet)
    

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

    <DayPilot:DayPilotScheduler runat="server"
      ...
      ViewType="Days"
      StartDate="2021-01-01"
      Days="31"
    />

### Tutorial

*   [Timesheet for ASP.NET (C#, VB.NET, SQL Server)](http://code.daypilot.org/60174/timesheet-for-asp-net-c-vb-net-sql-server)
    

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

    @Html.DayPilotScheduler("dps", new DayPilotSchedulerConfig {
      BackendUrl = ResolveUrl("~/Scheduler/Backend"),
      ...
      ViewType = DayPilot.Web.Mvc.Enums.Scheduler.ViewType.Gantt,
      StartDate = DateTime.Today,
      Days = 31
    })

### Tutorial

*   [ASP.NET MVC 5 Timesheet](http://code.daypilot.org/97360/asp-net-mvc-5-timesheet)
     (C#, VB)
    

AngularJS Timesheet
-------------------

    <!-- AngularJS -->
    <script src="http://ajax.googleapis.com/ajax/libs/angularjs/1.5.0/angular.min.js"></script>
    
    <!-- DayPilot -->
    <script src="js/daypilot/daypilot-all.min.js"></script>
    
    <!-- HTML -->
    <div ng-app="timesheetApp" ng-controller="TimesheetCtrl">
      <daypilot-scheduler id="dp" config="scheduler" events="events"></daypilot-scheduler>
    </div>
    
    
    <script>
    var app = angular.module('timesheetApp', ['daypilot']);
    
    app.controller('TimesheetCtrl', function($scope) {
    
        $scope.scheduler = {
            viewType: "Days",
            startDate: DayPilot.Date.today().firstDayOfMonth(),
            days: DayPilot.Date.today().daysInMonth(),
            cellWidthSpec: "Auto",
            businessBeginsHour: 9,
            businessEndsHour: 17,
            showNonBusiness: false
        };
        
        $scope.events = [\
          { id: 1, start: "2016-01-01T10:00:00", end: "2016-01-01T12:00:00", text: "Activity 1" }\
        ];
    });
    
    </script>

### Tutorial

    JavaScript, Angular, React and Vue

[![DayPilot](https://static.daypilot.org/img/logo/daypilot-logo-40x40.png)DayPilot](https://www.daypilot.org/)

[Products](https://www.daypilot.org/products/)

[Support](https://www.daypilot.org/support/)

  

[DayPilot Documentation](https://doc.daypilot.org/)

*   [Home](https://doc.daypilot.org/)
    *   [Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/ "Scheduler Component")
        *   [Appearance](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/appearance/ "Appearance")

Scheduler for Touch Devices
===========================

![javascript html5 scheduler touch ipad iphone android tablet](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/qo2uifgherdqjbt2dz2shtqwhu/javascript-html5-scheduler-touch-ipad-iphone-android-tablet.png "javascript html5 scheduler touch ipad iphone android tablet")

On mobile and touch devices, the Scheduler supports the following actions out of the box:

*   Event moving (moving is activated by press-and-hold gesture)
    
*   Event click (tap)
    
*   Time range selecting (tap to select a single cell or press-and-hold to start selecting multiple cells)
    

Event resizing is not mapped to any gesture. You can enable it by adding a custom icon using an [event active area](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/event-active-areas/ "Event Active Areas")
 with `action` set to `"ResizeEnd"`  or `"ResizeStart"` (see an example below).

Supported Platforms

*   iOS/iPadOS 12+ (iPhone, iPad)
    
*   Android 10+ (smart phones, tablets)
    

Tutorial
--------

*   [HTML5 Scheduler for Touch Devices - iPad, iPhone, Android](http://code.daypilot.org/84763/html5-scheduler-for-touch-devices-ipad-android-php-javascript)
     (PHP, JavaScript)
    

Custom Event Resizing Touch Handle
----------------------------------

![javascript html5 scheduler touch devices resizing](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/5vvzpe2o25bmranur7xfehykue/javascript-html5-scheduler-touch-devices-resizing.png "javascript html5 scheduler touch devices resizing")

This example displays an SVG icon (see [event icons](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/event-icons/ "Event Icons")
) at the right side of the Scheduler event box.

*   The `action` is set to `"ResizeEnd"`. This creates a resize handle (it works on both desktop and touch devices).
    
*   The icon will be permanently visible. You can hide it on desktop devices (it will only appear on mouse hover) by adding `visibility: "TouchVisible"` to the active area properties.
    

JavaScript Example

    onBeforeEventRender: args => {
      args.data.barHidden = true;
      args.data.backColor = "#6aa84f";
      args.data.fontColor = "#ffffff";
      args.data.borderColor = "darker";
      args.data.cssClass = "rounded";
      args.data.areas = [\
        {\
          right: 0,\
          top: 6,\
          height: 24,\
          width: 24,\
          symbol: "/assets/daypilot.svg#resize-right",\
          fontColor: "#ffffff",\
          action: "ResizeEnd"\
        }\
      ]
    }

Custom Context Menu Touch Icon
------------------------------

![javascript html5 scheduler touch devices context menu](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/d2pbzmr56bh75nqmz2ub7zfngq/javascript-html5-scheduler-touch-devices-context-menu.png "javascript html5 scheduler touch devices context menu")

JavaScript Example

    contextMenu: new DayPilot.Menu({
      // ...
    }),
    onBeforeEventRender: args => {
      args.data.barHidden = true;
      args.data.backColor = "#6aa84f";
      args.data.fontColor = "#ffffff";
      args.data.borderColor = "darker";
      args.data.cssClass = "rounded";
      args.data.areas = [\
        {\
          right: 0,\
          top: 6,\
          height: 24,\
          width: 24,\
          symbol: "/assets/daypilot.svg#minichevron-down-4",\
          fontColor: "#ffffff",\
          action: "ContextMenu"\
        }\
      ]
    }
    

Event "Tap-and-Hold" Gesture
----------------------------

By default, this gesture is mapped to activating event moving. As soon as the moving is activated the user can drag the event to a new position.

You can also switch the default action to a context menu using `eventTapAndHoldHandling` property.

The hold timeout is set to 300 ms. It can be customized using `tapAndHoldTimeout` property.

### JavaScript Example

    const scheduler = new DayPilot.Scheduler("scheduler", {
      tapAndHoldTimeout: 200,
      eventTapAndHoldHandling: "ContextMenu",
      contextMenu: new DayPilot.Menu({ /* ... */ }),
      // ...
    });
    scheduler.init();

Event Drag Handles
==================

The drag handles can be specified using the [moveBy](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-moveby/)
 property:

*   `"Full"` (default) - start dragging anywhere inside the event (no cursor indication), resizing drag handle on the left and on the right
    
*   `"Top"` - event moving drag handle on the top, resizing drag handle on the left and right
    
*   `"Left"` - event moving drag handle on the left, resizing drag handle on the right
    
*   `"None"` - no drag handles
    

When the value is set to `"None"` you can define a custom handle using [event active areas](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/event-active-areas/ "Event Active Areas")
.

### Drag Handle Size

 **EventMoveMargin** property:

![scheduler_event_move_margin281x81](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/ufnphsc2h5fozahf2nlucxwumy/scheduler_event_move_margin281x81.png "scheduler_event_move_margin281x81")

**EventResizeMargin** property:

![scheduler_event_resize_margin281x81](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/r5ivabhikzedhmc2sq7g7nbg3i/scheduler_event_resize_margin281x81.png "scheduler_event_resize_margin281x81")

JavaScript
----------

    <div id="dp"></div>
    <script type="text/javascript">
      var dp = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dp");
      dp.moveBy = "Full";
      // ...
      dp.init();
    </script>

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

Example:

    <DayPilot:DayPilotScheduler runat="server" id="DayPilotScheduler1"
      ...
      MoveBy = "Full"
    />

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

Example:

    @Html.DayPilotScheduler("dps", new DayPilotSchedulerConfig {
      BackendUrl = ResolveUrl("~/Scheduler/Backend"),
      ...
      MoveBy = DragArea.Full
    })

Event Selecting
===============

![javascript scheduler event selecting](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/a5bl3d7y3rhydd4st6ewk4gaye/javascript-scheduler-event-selecting.png "javascript scheduler event selecting")

### Enable event selecting

Event selecting can be mapped to the following user actions:

*   [onEventClick](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-oneventclick/)
    : to select events on click, set [eventClickHandling](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-eventclickhandling/)
     to `"Select"`
    
*   [onEventDoubleClick](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-oneventdoubleclick/)
    : to select events on double click, se eventDoubleClickHandling to select `"Select"`
    

### Selecting multiple events

Multiple event selection can be enabled using the the [allowMultiSelect](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-allowmultiselect/)
 property (by default, it is turned off).

*   When this feature is enabled, multiple events can be selected by the mapped action (e.g. event click) while holding the **Ctrl** key.
    

You can also use a [rectangle selection mode](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/rectangle-selection/ "Rectangle Selection")
 to select multiple events using **Shit + drag**.

### Highlighting the selected events (CSS)

The selected events will be marked with `*_selected` CSS class.

See also

*   [Scheduler CSS](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/css/ "CSS")
    

### Sample CSS style (scheduler\_white theme)

.css

    .scheduler_white_selected .scheduler_white_event_inner
    {
      background: #ddd;
    }

### Client-side API

You can select or unselect events manually using the client-side API.

*   [DayPilot.Scheduler.multiselect.add()](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-multiselect-add/)
    
*   [DayPilot.Scheduler.multiselect.clear](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-multiselect-clear/)
    ()
    
*   [DayPilot.Scheduler.multiselect.get](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-multiselect-get/)
    ()
    
*   [DayPilot.Scheduler.multiselect.redraw](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-multiselect-redraw/)
    ()
    
*   [DayPilot.Scheduler.multiselect.remove](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-multiselect-remove/)
    (e)
    

JavaScript
----------

Select an event using JavaScript:

    <div id="scheduler"></div>
    <script>
      const scheduler = new DayPilot.Scheduler("scheduler", {
        // ... config
      });
      
      scheduler.init();
    
      const selectedEvent = scheduler.events.find("123");
      scheduler.multiselect.add(selectedEvent);
    </script>

Enable [event multi-selecting](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/event-multi-selecting/ "Event Multi-Selecting")
 (using Ctrl + click):

    <div id="scheduler"></div>
    <script>
      const scheduler = new DayPilot.Scheduler("scheduler", {
        eventClickHandling: "Select",
        allowMultiSelect: true,
        // ...
      });
      scheduler.init();
    </script>

Prevent selection of certain events using the [onEventSelect](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-oneventselect/)
 event handler:

    <div id="scheduler"></div>
    <script>
      const scheduler = new DayPilot.Scheduler("scheduler", {
        onEventSelect: (args) => {
          // Prevent selecting events that contain the text "unselectable"
          if (args.selected && args.e.text().includes("unselectable")) {
            args.preventDefault();
          }
        },
        // ...
      });
    
      scheduler.init();
    </script>

Angular
-------

Select an event using a button click:

    import { Component, ViewChild } from '@angular/core';
    import { DayPilot, DayPilotSchedulerComponent } from 'daypilot-pro-angular';
    
    @Component({
      selector: 'app-scheduler-select',
      template: `
        <daypilot-scheduler 
          [config]="config"
          [events]="events">
        </daypilot-scheduler>
        <button (click)="selectEvent()">Select Event 123</button>
      `
    })
    export class SchedulerSelectComponent {
    
      @ViewChild(DayPilotSchedulerComponent)
      scheduler!: DayPilotSchedulerComponent;
    
      events: DayPilot.EventData[] = [\
        {\
          id: 123,\
          text: "Sample Event",\
          start: "2026-02-13T09:00:00",\
          end: "2026-02-13T10:00:00"\
        },\
        // ...\
      ];
    
      config: DayPilot.SchedulerConfig = {
        // Additional config options if needed
      };
    
      selectEvent(): void {
        const foundEvent = this.scheduler.control.events.find(123);
        if (foundEvent) {
          this.scheduler.control.multiselect.add(foundEvent);
        }
      }
    }
    

Enable [event multi-selecting](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/event-multi-selecting/ "Event Multi-Selecting")
 (using Ctrl + click):

    import { Component } from '@angular/core';
    import { DayPilot } from 'daypilot-pro-angular';
    
    @Component({
      selector: 'app-scheduler-multiselect',
      template: `
        <daypilot-scheduler
          [config]="config"
          [events]="events">
        </daypilot-scheduler>
      `
    })
    export class SchedulerMultiselectComponent {
    
      events: DayPilot.EventData[] = [\
        {\
          id: "1",\
          text: "Event 1",\
          start: "2026-02-13T09:00:00",\
          end: "2026-02-13T10:00:00"\
        },\
        {\
          id: "2",\
          text: "Event 2",\
          start: "2026-02-13T10:30:00",\
          end: "2026-02-13T11:30:00"\
        }\
      ];
    
      config: DayPilot.SchedulerConfig = {
        eventClickHandling: "Select",
        allowMultiSelect: true,
        // ...
      };
    
    }

Prevent selecting certain events:

    import { Component } from '@angular/core';
    import { DayPilot } from 'daypilot-pro-angular';
    
    @Component({
      selector: 'app-scheduler-noselect',
      template: `
        <daypilot-scheduler
          [config]="config"
          [events]="events">
        </daypilot-scheduler>
      `
    })
    export class SchedulerNoSelectComponent {
    
      events: DayPilot.EventData[] = [\
        {\
          id: "1",\
          text: "Normal Event",\
          start: "2026-02-13T09:00:00",\
          end: "2026-02-13T10:00:00"\
        },\
        {\
          id: "2",\
          text: "unselectable event",\
          start: "2026-02-13T10:30:00",\
          end: "2026-02-13T11:30:00"\
        }\
      ];
    
      config: DayPilot.SchedulerConfig = {
        onEventSelect: (args) => {
          // Prevent selecting events whose text includes "unselectable"
          if (args.selected && args.e.text().includes("unselectable")) {
            args.preventDefault();
          }
        },
        // ...
      };
    
    }

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

    <DayPilot:DayPilotScheduler runat="server" id="DayPilotScheduler1"
      ...
      EventClickHandling="Select"
      AllowMultiSelect="true"
    />

### EventSelect event

Whenever the user updates the selection, **EventSelect** can be fired. The handler must be enabled first using **EventSelectHandling** property. The default value of EventSelectHandling is Disabled.

### Server-side API

The list of selected events is available in **SelectedEvents** property. It is synchronized during PostBacks or CallBacks and is accessible in all event handlers (not only EventSelect).

### Demo

*   [http://www.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/EventSelecting.aspx](http://www.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/EventSelecting.aspx)
    

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

Set EventClickHandling to EventClickHandlingType.Select.

    @Html.DayPilotScheduler("dps", new DayPilotSchedulerConfig {
      BackendUrl = ResolveUrl("~/Scheduler/Backend"),
      ...
      EventClickHandling = EventClickHandlingType.Select,
      AllowMultiSelect = false
    })
    

### Select Event

You can handle the select event using OnEventSelect method on the server side by setting EventSelectHandling to EventSelectHandlingType.CallBack.

View

    @Html.DayPilotScheduler("dps", new DayPilotSchedulerConfig {
      BackendUrl = ResolveUrl("~/Scheduler/Backend"),
      ...
      EventClickHandling = EventClickHandlingType.Select,
      EventSelectHandling = EventSelectHandlingType.CallBack
    })
    

Backend Controller

    protected override void OnEventSelect(EventSelectArgs e)
    {
      if (e.Event.Value == "1" && e.Change == EventSelectChange.Selected)
      {
        SelectedEvents.Add(EventInfo.Create("13"));  // add another event to the selection when event 1 was selected
      }
      Update();
    }

### Demo

*   [http://mvc.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/EventSelecting](http://mvc.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/EventSelecting)

Client-Side Update
==================

Call update() on the client side to immediately update the calendar after changing the properties. This used to require a server-side request using commandCallBack().

### Changing Cell Height (Example)

dpc.cellHeight = 40;
dpc.update();

Availability
------------

Availability of this feature in DayPilot editions:

|     | Lite | Pro |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [DayPilot for JavaScript](https://javascript.daypilot.org/) |     |     |
| [DayPilot for ASP.NET WebForms](https://www.daypilot.org/) |     |     |
| [DayPilot for ASP.NET MVC](https://mvc.daypilot.org/) |     |     |

Client-Side Update
==================

Call update() on the client side to immediately update the calendar after changing the properties. This used to require a server-side request using commandCallBack().

### Example

dpm.showWeekend = false;
dpm.update();

------------

Dynamic Event Loading
=====================

![html5 scheduler dynamic event loading](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/wwxweiazkrfmndgd57xz6d7eya/html5-scheduler-dynamic-event-loading.png "html5 scheduler dynamic event loading")

The [JavaScript Scheduler](https://javascript.daypilot.org/scheduler/)
 can load events from the server dynamically as the user scrolls.

Notes
-----

*   The Scheduler fires `onScroll` event after every scrollbar position change.
    
*   The delay before the Scroll event is fired can be configured using `scrollDelayDynamic` property.
    

JavaScript Scheduler
--------------------

Handle the [onScroll](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-onscroll/)
 event to supply events for the viewport.

See also:

*   [DayPilot.Scheduler.dynamicLoading](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-dynamicloading/)
    
*   [DayPilot.Scheduler.onScroll](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-onscroll/)
    

### Example

    <script>
      const dp = new DayPilot.Scheduler("dp", {
        dynamicLoading: true,
        onScroll: async (args) => {
        
          const start = args.viewport.start;
          const end = args.viewport.end;
    
          const {data} = await DayPilot.Http.get(`/events?start=${start}&end=${end}`);
          args.events = data;
          args.loaded();
    
        ),
        // ...
      });
      dp.init();
    
    </script>

### JavaScript Tutorials

*   [HTML5 Scheduler with Dynamic Event Loading](https://code.daypilot.org/85715/html5-scheduler-with-dynamic-event-loading)
     (PHP backend)
    
*   [JavaScript Scheduler: Dynamic Event Loading](https://code.daypilot.org/71187/javascript-scheduler-dynamic-event-loading-asp-net-core)
     (ASP.NET Core Backend)
    

### Live Demo

*   [http://javascript.daypilot.org/demo/scheduler/dynamic.html](http://javascript.daypilot.org/demo/scheduler/dynamic.html)
    

Angular Scheduler
-----------------

This example shows how to enable dynamic event loading in the [Angular Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/angular/ "Angular")
 component.

### Example

    import {Component, ViewChild, AfterViewInit} from "@angular/core";
    import {DayPilot, DayPilotSchedulerComponent} from "daypilot-pro-angular";
    import {DataService} from "./data.service";
    
    @Component({
      selector: 'scheduler-component',
      template: `
        <daypilot-scheduler [config]="config" #scheduler></daypilot-scheduler>
      `,
      styles: [``]
    })
    export class SchedulerComponent implements AfterViewInit {
    
      @ViewChild("scheduler")
      scheduler!: DayPilotSchedulerComponent;
    
      config: any = {
        dynamicLoading: true,
        onScroll: args => {
          let dp = this.scheduler.control;
          let viewport = dp.getViewPort();
          let from = viewport.start;
          let to = viewport.end;
          let rstart = viewport.resources[0];
          let rend = viewport.resources[viewport.resources.length - 1];
    
          args.async = true;
          this.ds.getEventsForViewport(from, to, rstart, rend).subscribe(result => {
            args.events = result;
            args.loaded();
          });
        },
        // ...
      };
    
      constructor(private ds: DataService) {
      }
    
    }

### Angular Tutorial

*   [Angular Scheduler: Dynamic Loading of Large Data Sets](https://code.daypilot.org/15036/angular-scheduler-dynamic-loading-of-large-data-sets)
    

React Scheduler
---------------

In the [React Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/react/ "React")
 component, enable the dynamic event loading using `dynamicLoading` property and add an `onScroll` event handler that will supply event data for the new viewport.

In this example, we extend the viewport date range by one day in both directions to create a buffer with preloaded events. The list of resources included in the current viewport is sent as a comma-separated list of resource IDs.

    import React, {Component} from 'react';
    import {DayPilotScheduler} from "daypilot-pro-react";
    
    class Scheduler extends Component {
    
        constructor(props) {
            super(props);
    
            this.state = {
                dynamicLoading: true,
                onScroll: args => {
                  const viewport = args.viewport;
                  const from = viewport.start.addDays(-1);
                  const to = viewport.end.addDays(1);
                  const resources = viewport.resources.map(r => r.id);
                  const resourceList = resources.join(",");
    
                  const {data: events} = await DayPilot.Http.get(`/api/eventsForViewport?from=${from}&to=${to}&resources=${resourceList}`);
                  args.events = events;
                  args.loaded();                        
                }
            };
        }
    
    
        render() {
            return (
                <div>
                    <DayPilotScheduler
                        {...this.state}
                    />
                </div>
            );
        }
    }
    
    export default Scheduler;

Vue Scheduler
-------------

In the [Vue Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/vue-js/ "Vue")
 component, you can enable dynamic event loading using the `dynamicLoading` property of the `config` object. To load the events for the updated viewport during scrolling, use the `onScroll` event handler:

    <template>
      <DayPilotScheduler id="dp" :config="config" ref="scheduler" />
    </template>
    
    <script>
    import {DayPilot, DayPilotScheduler} from 'daypilot-pro-vue'
    
    export default {
      name: 'Scheduler',
      data: function() {
        return {
          config: {
            dynamicLoading: true,
            onScroll: async args => {
              const viewport = args.viewport;
              const from = viewport.start.addMonths(-1);
              const to = viewport.end.addMonths(1);
              const rstart = viewport.resources[0];
              const rend = viewport.resources[viewport.resources.length - 1];
    
              const {data: events} = await DayPilot.Http.get(`/api/backend_events_viewport.php?from=${from}&to=${to}&rstart=${rstart}&rend=${rend}`);
              args.events = events;
              args.loaded();
            },
            // ...
          },
        }
      },
      components: {
        DayPilotScheduler
      },
      // ...
    }
    </script>

### Vue Tutorial

*   [Vue Scheduler: On-Demand Event Loading](https://code.daypilot.org/42385/vue-scheduler-on-demand-event-loading)
    

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

By default, events for the **full grid** (defined by [StartDate](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-startdate/)
 and [Days](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-days/)
) are loaded from the server. The scheduler may become slow when displaying a large number of events.

There is an option to load only the events for the **visible range**. Events will be loaded and rendered as the users scrolls.

1\. Activate the dynamic event loading mode using DynamicLoading property"

    DynamicLoading="true"

2\. Handle **Scroll** event:

    OnScroll="DayPilotScheduler1_Scroll"
    
    protected void DayPilotScheduler1_Scroll(object sender, DayPilot.Web.Ui.Events.ScrollEventArgs e)
    {
      setDataSourceAndBind();
      DayPilotScheduler1.Update();
    }

Information about the visible range is stored in the **ViewPort** property:

*   ViewPort.Start - Start of the visible range (DateTime)
    
*   ViewPort.End - End of the visible range (DateTime)
    
*   ViewPort.Resources - List of IDs (Value property) of the visible resources (List<string>)
    

### Tutorial

*   [ASP.NET Scheduler with On-Demand Event Loading](http://code.daypilot.org/80052/asp-net-scheduler-with-on-demand-event-loading)
    

### Demo

*   [Demo: Dynamic Event Loading](http://www.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/DynamicEventLoading.aspx)
    

ASP.NET MVC
-----------

Events can be loaded dynamically (on demand) as the user scrolls.

### Enable Dynamic Event Loading

    DynamicEventLoading = true

### Load the Events upon Request

New events are requested from the server using **OnScroll** event every time the scrollbar position is changed.

Details about the new viewport are available in ViewPort property.

*   ViewPort.Start
    
*   ViewPort.End
    
*   ViewPort.Resources
    

### Example

View

    @Html.DayPilotScheduler("dps_dynamic", new DayPilotSchedulerConfig {
      BackendUrl = ResolveUrl("~/Scheduler/Backend"),
      ...
      DynamicLoading = true,
    })

Backend Controller

    protected override void OnScroll(ScrollArgs e)
    {
      DateTime start = ViewPort.Start;
      DateTime end = ViewPort.End;
      List<string> resources = ViewPort.Resources;
    
      Events = new DataManager().GetEvents(start, end, resources);
      Update(CallBackUpdateType.EventsOnly);
    }

### Demo

*   [http://mvc.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/DynamicEventLoading](http://mvc.daypilot.org/demo/Scheduler/DynamicEventLoading)

| [DayPilot for ASP.NET WebForms](https://www.daypilot.org/) |     |     |

            *   [Notify Queue](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/notify-queue/ "Notify Queue")

        *   [Navigation](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/navigation/ "Navigation")

        *   [View Types](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/view-types/ "View Types")
            

Notify Event Model
==================

![monthly event calendar ajax notify](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/5rxys7ios5ajjpu4h3cdkdqzt4/monthly-event-calendar-ajax-notify.png "monthly event calendar ajax notify")

Event handling models
---------------------

### Refresh event model

The traditional **refresh** model is used when event handling is set to CallBack (e.g. EventMoveHandling="CallBack") or PostBack (ASP.NET WebForms only).

1.  An action is performed by the user (event moving, resizing).
2.  Event handler is fired and the details are sent to the server side.
3.  The server-side event handler updates the database and reloads the events.
4.  Updated event set is sent to the client side.
5.  All events are refreshed on the client side.

### Notify event model

The fast **notify** refresh model is used when event handling is set to Notify (e.g. EventMoveHandling="Notify"). It can be used automatically for event moving (EventMoveHandling) and event resizing (EventResizeHandling). It can be also used for client side event updates.

1.  An action is performed by the user (event moving, resizing).
2.  The events are updated on the client side and the change is immediately visible.
3.  Event handler is fired and the details are sent to the server side.
4.  The server-side event handler updates the database. Client-side event refresh is optional.

Speed gains
-----------

The Notify model allows a super-fast UI feedback. Nothing has to be loaded from the server side and only the changed rows are redrawn.

While the same change may take **several seconds** using the traditional **refresh** model (server round-trip, events reloaded from a db, client-side reload) it will take only **a few milliseconds** with the **notify** model.

Notify event queue
------------------

Depending on the NotifyCommit property value the client-side changes can be sent to the server side immediately (NotifyCommit="Immediate") or they can be queued on the client side (NotifyCommit="Queue").

The queued events must be sent manually using client-side API:

dpc.queue.notify();

The "Notify" event model updates the client first and then notifies the server about the change. Compare this model with the traditional "Refresh" model where the change is first sent to the server, the server returns and updated event set and the client is refreshed.

Server-side event handlers
--------------------------

### EventMove, EventResize

Both event moving (EventMoveHandling="Notify") and event resizing (EventResizeHandling="Notify") fire the standard event handlers on the server side:

*   [EventMove](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/event-moving/ "Event Moving")
    
*   [EventResize](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/event-resizing/ "Event Resizing")
    

Example (ASP.NET WebForms):

protected void DayPilotMonth1\_EventMove(object sender, EventMoveEventArgs e)
{
  // update the database
  eventMove(e.Value, e.NewStart, e.NewEnd);

  // send a confirmation to the client
  DayPilotMonth1.UpdateWithMessage("Event moved.");
}

If you call UpdateWithMessage() or Update() without the CallBackUpdateType specified DayPilot Month will detect the Notify mode and it will not send the updated event set to the client.

It is possible to force client-side refresh using Update(CallBackUpdateType.EventsOnly) or Update(CallBackUpdateType.Full).

*   This might be necessary if the action is forbidden (i.e. the required action will not be stored and the events need to be reset to the original state).
*   Sometimes the change affects other events as well so they need to be updated.
*   You may want to update resource details (e.g. row summaries).

### EventAdd, EventRemove, EventUpdate

Individual actions submitted using [DayPilot.Action.notify()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-action-notify/)
 invoke **EventAdd**, **EventRemove**, or **EventUpdate** event handlers (depending on action type).

See also [Update Events without Reloading](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/update-events-without-reloading/ "Update Events without Reloading")

### Notify

The [Notify event handler](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/notify-event-handler/ "Notify Event Handler")
 is fired when the [action queue](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/notify-queue/ "Notify Queue")
 is submitted using [DayPilot.Month.queue.notify()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-queue-notify/)
.

# Client-Side Event API - Monthly Event Calendar | DayPilot Documentation - Scheduling Components for
    JavaScript, Angular, React and Vue

[![DayPilot](https://static.daypilot.org/img/logo/daypilot-logo-40x40.png)DayPilot](https://www.daypilot.org/)

[Products](https://www.daypilot.org/products/)

[Support](https://www.daypilot.org/support/)

  

[DayPilot Documentation](https://doc.daypilot.org/)

*   [Home](https://doc.daypilot.org/)
    *   [Monthly Event Calendar](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/ "Monthly Event Calendar Component")
        *   [AJAX](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/ajax/ "AJAX")
            
        *   [Appearance](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/appearance/ "Appearance")
            
        *   [Client-Side API](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/client-side-api/ "Client-Side API")
            *   [Client-Side Event API](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/client-side-event-api/ "Client-Side Event API")

        *   [Localization](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/localization/ "Localization")

        *   [Touch Devices](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/touch-devices/ "Touch Devices")
            
        *   [View Types](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/view-types/ "View Types")
            

Client-Side Event API
=====================

You can add, remove, and update events on the client side.

### Overview

*   [events.add](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-add/ "DayPilot.Month.events.add")
    (e, data)
*   [events.find](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-find/ "DayPilot.Month.events.find")
    (id)
*   [events.findRecurrent](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-findrecurrent/ "DayPilot.Month.events.findRecurrent")
    (masterId, time)
*   [events.remove](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-remove/ "DayPilot.Month.events.remove")
    (e, data)
*   [events.update](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-update/ "DayPilot.Month.events.update")
    (e, data)

### See also

*   [Update Events without Reloading](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/update-events-without-reloading/ "Update Events without Reloading")
    
*   [Notify Event Model](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/notify-event-model/ "Notify Event Model")
    

JavaScript
----------

### Event adding

Events can be added to the [Monthly Calendar](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/ "Monthly Event Calendar Component")
 using [events.add()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-add/)
 method.

var e = new DayPilot.Event({start:new DayPilot.Date(), end:(new DayPilot.Date()).addHours(5), value: DayPilot.guid(), text: "New Event"});
dpm.events.add(e);

### Event updating

Events can be updated using [events.update()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-update/)
 method. The object representing the event must be acquired using [events.find()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-find/)
 or [event.findRecurrent()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-findrecurrent/)
.

var e = dpc.events.find('123');  
dpc.text('New Event Name');  // update the event text  
dpc.client.innerHTML('New Event Name');  // update the event HTML
dpc.events.update(e);

### Event removing

Events can be removed using [events.remove()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-remove/)
 method. The object representing the event must be acquired using [events.find()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-find/)
 or [event.findRecurrent()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-findrecurrent/)
.

var e = dpc.events.find('123');
dpc.events.remove(e);

ASP.NET WebForms
----------------

### Event adding

Events can be added to the [Monthly Calendar](https://doc.daypilot.org/month/ "Monthly Event Calendar Component")
 using [events.add()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-add/)
 method.

var e = new DayPilot.Event({start:new DayPilot.Date(), end:(new DayPilot.Date()).addHours(5), value: DayPilot.guid(), text: "New Event"});
dpm.events.add(e).notify();

This example will add a new event called "New Event" and notify the server by firing **EventAdd** event.

protected void DayPilotMonth1\_EventAdd(object sender, EventAddEventArgs e)
{
  // create the event in the database using custom method
  createEvent(e.Event.Value, e.Event.Start, e.Event.End, e.Event.Text);  // your method for updating the database

  // display a message to the user
  DayPilotMonth1.UpdateWithMessage("Added.");
}

### Event updating

Events can be updated using [events.update()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-update/)
 method. The object representing the event must be acquired using [events.find()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-find/)
 or [event.findRecurrent()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-findrecurrent/)
.

var e = dpc.events.find('123');
dpm.text('New Event Name');  // update the event text
dpm.events.update(e).notify();

This example will find an event with id "123", update the text and displayed HTML, and notify the server by firing **EventUpdate** event.

protected void DayPilotMonth1\_EventUpdate(object sender, EventUpdateEventArgs e)
{
  createEvent(e.Event.Value, e.Event.Start, e.Event.End, e.Event.Text); // your method for updating the database

  // display a message to the user
  DayPilotMonth1.UpdateWithMessage("Updated.");
}

### Event removing

Events can be removed using [events.remove()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-remove/)
 method. The object representing the event must be acquired using [events.find()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-find/)
 or [event.findRecurrent()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-month-events-findrecurrent/)
.

var e = dpc.events.find('123');
dpm.events.remove(e).notify();

This example will find an event with id "123", remove it, and notify the server by firing **EventRemove** event.

protected void DayPilotMonth1\_EventRemove(object sender, EventRemoveEventArgs e)  
{  
  removeEvent(e.Event.Value);  // your method for updating the database  
  
  // display a message to the user
  DayPilotMonth1.UpdateWithMessage("Deleted.");
}

Notify Event Model
==================

![event calendar ajax notify](https://cdn.doc.daypilot.org/page/image/hihlyxitofbefdalnll6okl5vm/event-calendar-ajax-notify.png "event calendar ajax notify")

Event handling models
---------------------

### Refresh event model

The traditional **refresh** model is used when event handling is set to CallBack (e.g. EventMoveHandling="CallBack") or PostBack (ASP.NET WebForms only).

1.  An action is performed by the user (event moving, resizing).
2.  Event handler is fired and the details are sent to the server side.
3.  The server-side event handler updates the database and reloads the events.
4.  Updated event set is sent to the client side.
5.  All events are refreshed on the client side.

### Notify event model

The fast **notify** refresh model is used when event handling is set to Notify (e.g. EventMoveHandling="Notify"). It can be used automatically for event moving (EventMoveHandling) and event resizing (EventResizeHandling). It can be also used for client side event updates.

1.  An action is performed by the user (event moving, resizing).
2.  The events are updated on the client side and the change is immediately visible.
3.  Event handler is fired and the details are sent to the server side.
4.  The server-side event handler updates the database. Client-side event refresh is optional.

Speed gains
-----------

The Notify model allows a super-fast UI feedback. Nothing has to be loaded from the server side and only the changed rows are redrawn.

While the same change may take **several seconds** using the traditional **refresh** model (server round-trip, events reloaded from a db, client-side reload) it will take only **a few milliseconds** with the **notify** model.

Notify event queue
------------------

Depending on the NotifyCommit property value the client-side changes can be sent to the server side immediately (NotifyCommit="Immediate") or they can be queued on the client side (NotifyCommit="Queue").

The queued events must be sent manually using client-side API:

dpc.queue.notify();

The "Notify" event model updates the client first and then notifies the server about the change. Compare this model with the traditional "Refresh" model where the change is first sent to the server, the server returns and updated event set and the client is refreshed.

Server-side event handlers
--------------------------

### EventMove, EventResize

Both event moving (EventMoveHandling="Notify") and event resizing (EventResizeHandling="Notify") fire the standard event handlers on the server side:

*   [EventMove](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/event-moving/ "Event Moving")
    
*   [EventResize](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/event-resizing/ "Event Resizing")
    

Example (ASP.NET WebForms):

protected void DayPilotCalendar1\_EventMove(object sender, EventMoveEventArgs e)
{
  // update the database
  eventMove(e.Value, e.NewStart, e.NewEnd);

  // send a confirmation to the client
  DayPilotCalendar1.UpdateWithMessage("Event moved.");
}

If you call UpdateWithMessage() or Update() without the CallBackUpdateType specified DayPilot Calendar will detect the Notify mode and it will not send the updated event set to the client.

It is possible to force client-side refresh using Update(CallBackUpdateType.EventsOnly) or Update(CallBackUpdateType.Full).

*   This might be necessary if the action is forbidden (i.e. the required action will not be stored and the events need to be reset to the original state).
*   Sometimes the change affects other events as well so they need to be updated.
*   You may want to update resource details (e.g. row summaries).

### EventAdd, EventRemove, EventUpdate

Individual actions submitted using [DayPilot.Action.notify()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-action-notify/)
 invoke **EventAdd**, **EventRemove**, or **EventUpdate** event handlers (depending on action type).

See also [Update Events without Reloading](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/update-events-without-reloading/ "Update Events without Reloading")

### Notify

The [Notify event handler](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/notify-event-handler/ "Notify Event Handler")
 is fired when the [action queue](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/notify-queue/ "Notify Queue")
 is submitted using [DayPilot.Calendar.queue.notify()](http://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-calendar-queue-notify/)
.

# Client-Side Update - Scheduler | DayPilot Documentation - Scheduling Components for
    JavaScript, Angular, React and Vue

[![DayPilot](https://static.daypilot.org/img/logo/daypilot-logo-40x40.png)DayPilot](https://www.daypilot.org/)

[Products](https://www.daypilot.org/products/)

[Support](https://www.daypilot.org/support/)

  

[DayPilot Documentation](https://doc.daypilot.org/)

*   [Home](https://doc.daypilot.org/)
    *   [Scheduler](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/ "Scheduler Component")
        *   [Appearance](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/appearance/ "Appearance")
            
        *   [Client-Side API](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/client-side-api/ "Client-Side API")
            *   [Client-Side Update](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/client-side-update/ "Client-Side Update")
                
            *   [Client-Side Event API](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/client-side-event-api/ "Client-Side Event API")
                
        *   [Controls](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/controls/ "Controls")
            
        *   [CSS](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/css/ "CSS")
            
        *   [Events](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/events/ "Events")

Client-Side Update
==================

Call [update](https://api.daypilot.org/daypilot-scheduler-update/)
() on the client side to update the Scheduler after changing the properties.

For a server-side assisted update, please use [CommandCallBack](https://doc.daypilot.org/scheduler/commandcallback/ "CommandCallBack()")
 (ASP.NET WebForms, ASP.NET MVC, Java versions).

### Changing a Date (Example)

    dps.startDate = "2025-05-01";
    dps.update();

Remember to have the events preloaded or to load them manually using events.add().

# Client-Side Event API - Event Calendar | DayPilot Documentation - Scheduling Components for
    JavaScript, Angular, React and Vue

[![DayPilot](https://static.daypilot.org/img/logo/daypilot-logo-40x40.png)DayPilot](https://www.daypilot.org/)

[Products](https://www.daypilot.org/products/)

[Support](https://www.daypilot.org/support/)

  

[DayPilot Documentation](https://doc.daypilot.org/)

*   [Home](https://doc.daypilot.org/)
    *   [Event Calendar](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/ "Event Calendar Component")
        *   [Appearance](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/appearance/ "Appearance")
            
        *   [Client-Side API](https://doc.daypilot.org/calendar/client-side-api/ "Client-Side API")
